description: The DESI survey examined the motion of galaxies and supernovae, testing the constancy of dark energy. Faint signals point to its evolution, and the Mirage model gave the best but not definitive result. If the energy is indeed growing, the Universe could face a tearing apart of matter.
abstract: Analysis of DESI DR2 data (a spectroscopic survey) combined with Type Ia supernovae and the cosmic microwave background has put the idea of an evolving dark energy to the test. Different models were tested, where dark energy density is described by parameters w₀ and wₐ. The trail of clues pointing to evolution was led mainly by LRG1-2 tracer galaxies, but the verdict remains fuzzy and statistically weak (significance 1.1–2.3 σ). Curiously, most models predict a 'phantom crossing' at a redshift of ~0.5 — as if dark energy switched gears halfway through. But it's still too early to claim a discovery.
threads: Новые наблюдения миллионов галактик показали: тёмная сила, ускоряющая Вселенную, возможно, не постоянна. Её поведение напоминает двигатель, который то затихает, то разгоняется. Если это так, физику ждет переворот.

title: Atoms in a Row: A New Way to Control Light
oneliner: Physicists arranged single atoms into a tidy chain, transforming them into a comb for light waves.
description: By cooling atoms to near absolute zero and arranging them with laser tweezers, scientists created a ruler that directs light into a narrow beam. The more atoms in the row, the sharper the beam. An effect previously known only in crystals made of billions of atoms emerged for the first time in just a
abstract: By combining atomic traps (optical tweezers) with a ring resonator and chiral coupling, scientists obtained highly directional Bragg reflection from a chain of atoms. With each added atom, the reflected beam became sharper—like increasing the number of lines on a diffraction grating. This was achieved thanks to laser cooling to near the ground state (phonon numbers 0.17 and 3.4). The platform combines controlled atomic arrays with strong light-matter coupling, which is important for quantum computing and metrology.
threads: Ученые выстроили атомы в линию с помощью лазерных пинцетов и заставили свет отражаться от них, как от зеркала. Увеличивая число атомов, пучок становился все уже. Теперь можно конструировать квантовые схемы на заказ.

title: Sunlight replaces lasers: quantum imaging without complex optics
oneliner: Physicists have learned to split sunlight into pairs of quantum twins and obtained an image for the first time using only solar illumination instead of lasers.
description: Sunlight is passed through a crystal that splits each photon into two entangled halves. Their invisible link allows imaging where ordinary optics are blind: in fog, under murky water, in near-total darkness. Previously, only lasers could do this—bulky and expensive. Now, daylight can be the source,
abstract: The work demonstrates for the first time that sunlight can serve as a pump for spontaneous parametric down-conversion, creating photon pairs with positional correlation. Such pairs are sufficient for quantum imaging—obtaining images with advantages over classical optics. Just as crowd noise can coalesce into a phrase, chaotic sunlight creates ordered quantum states. This discovery will allow scattered light to be used in quantum information systems, such as on satellites, eliminating the need for lasers.
threads: Обычный солнечный свет разделили на пары фотонов-близнецов, которые «чувствуют» друг друга на расстоянии. Это позволило сделать квантовый снимок без лазера. Теперь уличный свет или даже свет далёких звёзд может заменить сложную оптику в квантовых камерах. Космические телескопы будущего, возможно, будут обходиться одним солнечным зайчиком.

title: Why Dark Molecular States Promise Better Solar Cells
oneliner: Scientists have discovered that built-in charge imbalances in molecules create a hidden pathway for light, giving rise to dark states with remarkable stability.
description: Many organic molecules have a built-in charge imbalance that changes upon light absorption. In pairs of such molecules, this opens hidden channels between bright and dark states. The discovered dark states barely respond to thermal noise, promising more efficient carbon-based solar cells.
abstract: In a pair of molecules (a dimer), permanent dipoles that depend on the electronic state enable optical transitions between bright and dark states. Static interactions let excited states indirectly talk to the unexcited one, and through interference of permanent and transition dipoles, they create highly localized dark states. These states show enhanced robustness to energy fluctuations, which could improve the design of photovoltaic devices.
threads: Внутри органических молекул прячутся «наклонённые» заряды — постоянные диполи. Обычно их не замечают, но они умеют открывать тайные ходы для света. Благодаря этому рождаются сверхстойкие «тёмные» состояния, способные улучшить солнечные батареи будущего.

title: Speed Outsmarts the Laws of Heat Engines
oneliner: Moving near light speed lets quantum sensors squeeze more thermal energy, breaking old efficiency limits.
description: A heat engine made of quantum particles, flying almost as fast as light, perceives temperature in a whole new way. Einstein’s effects make the hot even hotter and the cold even colder, boosting useful work and bypassing the classical barrier. Speed becomes a thermodynamic trump card.
abstract: Scientists have studied a quantum heat engine whose working medium is two qubits interacting with a radiation field at different temperatures. When the qubits start moving, due to relativistic effects they sense temperatures different from those of stationary thermometers — this is analogous to the well-known Unruh effect. This difference in 'apparent' temperatures becomes a resource: the authors showed that the engine can produce more work and achieve efficiency beyond the standard Carnot limit calculated for bodies at rest. Like a sailboat harnessing the apparent wind, the engine draws energy from motion.
threads: Скорость — тоже источник энергии. Квантовый тепловой двигатель, летящий почти со скоростью света, «видит» температуру иначе и может извлекать больше работы, чем ему положено. Когда-нибудь так смогут питаться зонды в межзвёздном пространстве.

title: Diamond DNA Chip: A Revolution in Rapid Diagnostics
oneliner: This diamond chip needs no chemistry: it senses 49 disease markers by their magnetic whispers.
description: A diamond plate with 49 DNA sensors catches disease marker molecules. When the target is caught, the magnetic noise quiets, and the sensor gives a signal—fast, with no reagents or bulky equipment.
abstract: Researchers have for the first time integrated a DNA microarray onto a diamond plate with NV centers, creating a platform for parallel quantum detection of 49 biomolecules. Each spot on the chip recognizes its target, and upon binding, a quantum mechanical effect is triggered: displacement of a chain bearing gadolinium ions increases the spin relaxation time, generating a clear signal. This architecture solves the long-standing problem of combining high sensor density with biochemical accuracy and contamination resistance. The technology resembles an orchestra, where each instrument plays only when a specific molecule appears, creating a multidimensional picture of the sample's composition.
threads: В алмазе, таком же как в кольце, спрятаны квантовые магнитные стрелки. Они «ломаются» от магнитных «помех». Учёные нанесли на алмаз кусочки ДНК, которые приманивают магнитные метки. Когда в капле крови нужная молекула убирает метку, стрелка выпрямляется — и датчик срабатывает. Такой чип размером с ноготь может искать сразу 49 болезней без пробирок и реактивов.

title: Black Mirrors: How Gravitational Waves Will Reveal Black Hole Twins
oneliner: Black mirrors — the twins of black holes — make gravitational waves sound different, and this can be heard.
description: One reflects, the other swallows. Black holes and black mirrors alter the music of gravitational waves, and future detectors will tell them apart by the rhythm of falling stars.
abstract: Classic black holes face problems with singularities and the information paradox. An alternative is the black mirror model, where the event horizon reflects rather than absorbs. Gravitational waves can reveal a key difference: the spectrum of quasinormal modes (the "ringing" after a merger) is fundamentally different. The reflectivity of the mirror horizon is universal and depends only on the Hawking temperature. This changes the dynamics of objects spiraling in: at low spin, braking slows the process, while at high spin, superradiance vanishes and the inspiral accelerates. This mechanism explains the growth of supermassive black holes to high spin values.
threads: Казалось бы, чёрная дыра — бездонная пропасть. Но что, если она похожа на идеальное зеркало, отражающее всё обратно? Такие «чёрные зеркала» меняют музыку гравитационных волн: падающие к ним звёзды звучат иначе. Разница в темпе сближения раскроет, кто есть кто, когда заработают новые детекторы.

title: Motion as Fuel: Quantum Engines at Near-Light Speeds
oneliner: Near-light-speed motion of reservoirs allows a quantum heat engine to operate with efficiency above the theoretical maximum.
description: Researchers have shown that if the heat and cold sources in a quantum maser (microwave amplifier) race along at nearly the speed of light, this engine circumvents the classical efficiency limit. Moreover, it can operate even without a temperature difference—the motion itself serves as fuel.
abstract: Scientists studied a quantum heat engine based on a three-level maser, where the reservoirs move at relativistic speeds. Due to the Doppler effect, their spectra are distorted, altering energy exchange. This allows exceeding the Carnot cycle efficiency (the theoretical maximum) at finite power and even extracting useful work without a temperature difference — motion itself becomes a source of thermodynamic advantage. It's as if the color of light changed due to source motion, opening up new energy states.
threads: Когда резервуары квантового двигателя мчатся почти со скоростью света, их тепловое излучение сдвигается по частоте. Это позволяет машине работать эффективнее дозволенного классической термодинамикой предела и даже получать энергию без разницы температур. Релятивистское движение становится новым топливом.

title: Time won't disappear when the stars go out
oneliner: The growth of disorder explains the flow of time, but not time itself.
description: Many think time is movement from order to chaos. But physicists found out: even when the Universe cools and everything freezes, time will remain. It will become a river flowing into an ocean: the current is gone, but the water is there. Only the difference between past and future will disappear.
abstract: The work points to a categorical mistake: time and entropy are not the same thing. Entropy (a measure of disorder) sets the arrow of time, but doesn't create time itself. Even at heat death (maximum entropy), temporal structure persists thanks to correlations in the quantum vacuum and the geometry of spacetime. It’s like a movie: even if the film stops rolling, the frame itself doesn't vanish—we simply lose direction. The twilight of time isn't the darkness of non-existence, but a loss of orientation.
threads: Считается, что время «умрёт» вместе со звёздами, когда беспорядок достигнет пика. Но новая работа объясняет: время никуда не денется, просто потеряет направление. Как река, впавшая в океан: течения больше нет, а вода — есть. Значит, у вечности нет стрелок, но она длится.

title: Tiny chip tames quantum entanglement
oneliner: Scientists have built a source of entangled light particles into a fingernail-sized chip powered by a regular battery.
description: The device generates pairs of entangled photons at a record rate—45 billion per second—consuming just milliwatts of power. The purity of the quantum connection exceeds 96%. Its tiny size and efficiency bring quantum internet, secure satellite links, and ultra-precise sensors closer to reality.
abstract: For the first time, researchers have created an electrically powered, fully chip-integrated source of polarization-entangled photons without the need for post-selection (filtering out unwanted states). They achieved this by hybrid integration of a miniature DFB laser and a thin-film lithium niobate chip with built-in periodically poled waveguides, a beam splitter, and a polarization converter. The resulting device boasts a record bandwidth of 73 nanometers and churns out up to 45 billion pairs per second per milliwatt of pump power; entanglement fidelity exceeds 96%. This integration rivals bulky lab setups and puts the key element of quantum communications literally at your fingertip.
threads: Если подбросить монетку, она упадёт орлом или решкой. А у фотонов всё иначе: измерение одного мгновенно определяет состояние другого, где бы он ни находился. Новый чип генерирует миллиарды таких пар в секунду и ускоряет создание защищённых квантовых сетей.

title: Entangled Photons vs. Murky Media
oneliner: Scientists used quantum entanglement to transmit images through scattering media that completely obscure ordinary light.
description: A new method uses pairs of entangled photons to transmit sharp images through murky media—places where ordinary light is helpless. The medium remains opaque to everyone except the quantum duo, opening the door to ultra-secure communication and improved medical and astronomical imaging.
abstract: When passing through turbid media like fog or biological tissue, light scatters and images are lost. Conventional methods try to undo the scattering but ignore light's quantum nature. This work creates a “quantum-classical filter”: using a wavefront and entangled photon pairs (particles whose correlations surpass any classical ones), images are transmitted clearly only for the quantum signal, while classical light gets completely scattered. It's like two synchronized dancers holding hands and moving through a crowd—they make it while a lone person gets lost. This approach paves the way for secure information transmission, where the channel stays opaque to ordinary signals but preserves the quantum link.
threads: Обычный свет в тумане — хаос. Но пара квантово-запутанных фотонов проходит мутную среду, как нож сквозь масло: картинка чёткая, а для чужих — только шум. Учёные превратили рассеяние в фильтр, открыв путь к неуязвимой связи.

title: Microwave Teleportation Withstands the Heat
oneliner: Quantum internet without total deep-freeze: teleportation of microwave signals achieved for the first time in a 'hot' channel at 4 K.
description: Connecting superconducting quantum computers is hindered by lethal heat. The solution is teleportation via entangled signals. In an experiment, the channel was heated to 4 K, yet fidelity remained above the classical limit. Now, distributed quantum networks can be built without cooling everything do
abstract: Superconducting quantum circuits demand ultra-low temperatures, but linking them directly is tough because of thermal noise in the channel. A new study demonstrates quantum teleportation of microwave coherent states between two cryostats over a channel as warm as 4 K. Using entangled pairs, scientists achieved 72.3% fidelity at 1 K and 59.9% at 4 K—beating the classical limit. Interestingly, the main culprit wasn't channel noise but parasitic heating of the equipment itself. This experiment shows that distributed quantum computing is possible even in imperfect conditions.
threads: Квантовые компьютеры любят холод. Но как их соединить, если любой провод несёт смертельное тепло? Учёные применили квантовую телепортацию и отправили микроволновый сигнал между холодильниками по «горячему» каналу при 4 К. Даже в таких адских (по квантовым меркам) условиях точность передачи побила классический рекорд. Значит, квантовый интернет можно строить без тотальной заморозки.

title: Fast Quantum Links: Delivered Before They Melt
oneliner: A new protocol instantly assembles quantum pairs, like a courier delivering ice cream in portions, and cuts noise by 45%.
description: Quantum entanglement is the fragile foundation of the future internet. It melts from noise like ice cream in the heat. Waiting for all parts only accelerates the melting. A new scheme processes each link immediately, leaving a minimal core. This nearly halves errors, paving the way for quantum netwo
abstract: A protocol for distributing multipartite entanglement via a quantum switch is proposed: instead of waiting for all Bell pairs (basic entangled states), it processes each one immediately, like an assembly line, reducing accumulated noise. Theoretically, the method relies on vertex covers in graph states. Simulations of systems up to 50 qubits showed that the protocol always achieves fidelity no lower than the baseline, and can reduce error by 45%. This is critical for exceeding the entanglement threshold in real-world quantum networks.
threads: Квантовые связи подобны мороженому — тают от тепла шума. Представьте курьера, который не ждёт полный заказ, а сразу доставляет порции, смешивая их в идеальное блюдо. Такой трюк снижает порчу на 45% и делает многостороннюю квантовую сеть реальнее.

title: Time Crystals Boost Batteries and Sensors
oneliner: Chains of magnets oscillating in their own rhythm can store energy and measure time with precision beyond the quantum limit.
description: A chain of tiny magnets, where coupling strength drops sharply with distance, under rhythmic kicks transitions into a discrete time crystal state. Energy accumulates avalanche-like, and sensitivity to rhythm disruptions surpasses all known limits.
abstract: Scientists built a model of a chain of quantum particles whose interaction decays according to a power law, and subjected it to periodic kicks. This led to a discrete time crystal phase — a kind of quantum pendulum that swings with a doubled period and accumulates energy faster than linearly. At the same time, the system becomes a sensitive sensor: the accuracy of estimating time shifts surpasses the standard Heisenberg limit, with the degree of superiority tuned by interaction parameters. The discovered platform promises new ways to store energy and measure time at the quantum level.
threads: Кристаллы времени: цепочки магнитов, колеблющиеся в два раза медленнее, чем их толкают. Оказалось, они могут стать идеальными квантовыми батареями — запас энергии растет быстрее с числом звеньев. А как датчики они бьют рекорды точности, превосходя квантовый предел.

title: Quantum Glue for the Universe
oneliner: Quantum condensates could explain why galaxies don't fly apart.
description: Physicists have found that in the extreme gravity of neutron stars and black holes, a Bose–Einstein condensate emerges — a quantum state where particles merge into a single whole, like droplets of mercury. If dark matter consists of ultra-light axions, it naturally turns into a cosmic glue that keep
abstract: Theoretical research shows that Bose–Einstein condensates (quantum states where many particles merge into a single wave) arise in ultra-strong gravitational fields, such as in neutron stars. The critical condensation temperature is not just a constant—it changes with the curvature of spacetime, which is important for understanding the early universe. If dark matter consists of axions, it forms a cosmic condensate with a coherence length on the order of light-days, naturally explaining the observed galaxy profiles. Quantum order might be everywhere in the universe.
threads: Квантовый конденсат может скреплять галактики. Частицы тёмной материи, сливаясь в одну волну-великана, не дают им развалиться и решают многолетнюю загадку астрономов. Кривизна пространства от тяжёлых объектов только помогает этому процессу.

title: Heat that Entangles: Diamond Defects
oneliner: Usually heat destroys quantum bonds, but here it creates them.
description: Physicists have found a way to entangle two microscopic defects in diamond simply by heating them. Usually heat destroys everything, but here it establishes quantum order instead. The secret is to make the environment behave like a crowd with uneven rules, which synchronizes the defects. This method
abstract: In quantum physics, heat usually disrupts the creation of entangled states—a special connection between particles. The authors propose a new approach: using a dissipative environment (a medium that scatters energy) with long-range correlations, one can drive a system to stationary entanglement without external control. The secret lies in breaking a conservation law during exchange with the environment: the system experiences competing heat flows, as if it were in contact with two different thermostats. As an example, they consider two defects in diamond, linked through a magnetized medium. This discovery points to a new mechanism for creating quantum links that requires no complex tuning.
threads: Обычно тепло — враг квантовой магии: малейший нагрев разрушает запутанность. Но физики придумали обратное: они нагревают алмазные дефекты, чтобы те сами сплетались в квантовый узел. Для этого они «обманывают» законы сохранения, заставляя среду толкать частицы в нужном ритме. В итоге рождаются устойчивые квантовые связи — без лазеров и дирижёров.

title: Search for alien radio signals along the planetary plane
oneliner: Earthly transmissions have shown: to catch an alien signal, you need to look where planets line up.
description: Analysis of 20 years of NASA antenna operations shows that the most powerful terrestrial radio signals are emitted in the plane of the Solar System. If extraterrestrial civilizations follow the same logic, their transmissions should be sought right there, especially when a planet passes in front of
abstract: An analysis of 20 years of NASA Deep Space Network logs revealed that Earth's radio signals mostly beam along the ecliptic plane and during planetary conjunctions. In the Earth transit zone, transmission frequency is 20 times above average. For an observer on Mars during conjunction, the chance of catching a signal hits 77% — 400,000 times higher than random. Like a lighthouse, we send out narrow beams, not a uniform hum. So for successful SETI, we should target exoplanet systems edge-on and time observations with planetary transits.
threads: Земные антенны посылают сигналы вдоль одной плоскости — там, где планеты. Если инопланетяне подслушивают в нужный момент, шанс поймать нас вырастает в 400 000 раз. Значит, и нам искать их стоит там же, в моменты планетных транзитов.

title: Interstellar Visitor with Carbonated Breath
oneliner: An object from another star system releases carbon dioxide like an open soda bottle, hiding its core in a cloud of dust.
description: The SPHEREx telescope split the light of the interstellar wanderer 3I ATLAS into colors. It revealed that it's actively emitting carbon dioxide, while water ice absorbs some of the rays like tinted glass. All visible glow is a dusty shell, with the solid core inside being hundreds of times smaller.
abstract: In August 2025, using the SPHEREx space telescope and the ground-based IRTF instrument, scientists managed to peek into the composition of the interstellar object 3I ATLAS. The spectra revealed water ice and powerful carbon dioxide emission: its output was around 9.4×10²⁶ molecules per second. This creates a vast gas envelope that, as it turned out, provides more than 99% of the observed light — like a glowing halo around a barely visible nucleus. The true nucleus might be only a few kilometers across, making it a typical cometary body rather than a large asteroid.
threads: Межзвёздный объект 3I ATLAS оказался окружён гигантским облаком из углекислого газа и пыли. Его ядро меньше 3 км, но выглядит как огромный снежный ком. Это искажает наши представления о пришельцах из других звёзд.

title: Heat capacity catches quantum entanglement in time
oneliner: A simple measurement of thermal response revealed a quantum link between past and future.
description: Temperature lag during heating isn't just physics—it's a manifestation of temporal quantum entanglement. That's when events at different times influence each other, as if past and present were sharing information. Researchers proved: if thermal inertia is large, then such an unusual link exists with
abstract: Physicists have found a way to certify time-entanglement using the imaginary part of heat capacity, measurable via calorimetry. This quantity, like an echo delay, points to temporal quantum connections. Linking to the pseudo-density matrix revealed nonclassical correlations through negativity and violation of temporal CHSH inequalities. Experimentally achievable bounds guaranteeing time-entanglement at the macrolevel have been found. This opens a straightforward path to searching for temporal quantum effects in condensed matter systems.
threads: Обычный тепловой отклик вещества хранит скрытое послание из прошлого. Физики показали, что, измеряя мнимую теплоёмкость, можно увидеть квантовую запутанность между событиями в разное время. Это как если бы суп в кастрюле помнил, как его мешали, и влиял на нагрев. Шаг к макроскопическим квантовым эффектам в повседневности.

title: Magnetic Brakes for Cosmic Spinning Tops
oneliner: How magnetic fields, like brakes, control the mass and spin of black holes, explaining the strange findings of gravitational wave detectors.
description: Scientists simulated the birth of black holes from massive stars and saw that magnetic fields act as a cosmic brake. A strong field slows the hole's spin, a weak one spins it up to near-light speed. This explained why detectors occasionally see pairs of heavyweight black holes, like the record-break
abstract: New simulations show that rotation and magnetic fields in massive low-metallicity stars explain the birth of black holes in the pair-instability mass gap (a mass range where ordinary stars can't form a black hole). Strong magnetic fields carry away angular momentum through outflows, suppressing accretion and leaving the black hole slowly spinning. Weak fields allow near-total collapse and spin-up to extreme rotation. Moderate fields naturally lead to parameters like those of GW231123 and GW190521. Possibly, this process gives birth to jets comparable to the most energetic gamma-ray bursts.
threads: Две чёрные дыры сливаются, каждая с массой и вращением, которые не должны существовать. Новые симуляции показывают: магнитные поля как космические тормоза — сильные поля замедляют вращение, слабые раскручивают дыру до немыслимых скоростей. Открытие объясняет самую тяжёлую гравитационно-волновую находку.

title: Precise tuning of quantum gates without compromising readout
oneliner: Physicists achieved record precision in all key operations of a quantum processor simultaneously.
description: A new tuning technique made it possible to combine ultra-precise single-qubit and two-qubit operations with high-quality readout. Previously, one harmed the other, but now a balance has been found. This is an important step toward computers that can correct their own errors.
abstract: In a superconducting quantum chip with two qubits (transmons) connected by a tunable bridge, they managed to simultaneously achieve record fidelity for the main operations: the two-qubit CZ gate at 99.93%, single-qubit gates at 99.98%, and readout above 99.94%. The key is fine-tuning the coupling strength, a new calibration protocol called PALEA, and a compatible readout scheme. It's like a race car where the engine, brakes, and steering all work flawlessly together. This approach paves the way toward scalable quantum processors without loss of quality.
threads: Обычно квантовый процессор похож на капризный оркестр: настройка скрипок заглушает виолончели. Но физики нашли способ дирижировать так, что все инструменты звучат чисто. В одной микросхеме достигли точности 99,98% для одиночных нот и 99,93% для дуэтов. Такой 'квантовый ансамбль' открывает путь к компьютерам, которые смогут исправлять свои ошибки.

title: Interstellar Visitors: Searching for Alien Technology
oneliner: Scientists are preparing to greet interstellar guests—and search them for signs of alien technology.
description: The Vera Rubin Telescope will discover thousands of interstellar visitors. To tell a natural object from a relic of another civilization, scientists have developed a protocol: they’ll study the light 'fingerprints' of these guests.
abstract: With the discovery of the third confirmed interstellar object, 3I/ATLAS, a new stage begins in the search for technosignatures—signs of extraterrestrial civilizations. Soon, the Vera C. Rubin Observatory will start the LSST survey, which will make detecting such “visitors” routine. The article reviews the literature: interstellar objects have long been considered ideal targets for SETI. Four classes of technosignatures (from shape anomalies to radiation) are identified, each with the data type and best observation time specified. An interesting fact: the first such object, ‘Oumuamua, was initially mistaken for an asteroid, but its strange acceleration led scientists to consider an artificial origin.
threads: Межзвёздные странники вроде Оумуамуа могут быть не просто глыбами льда и камня, а зондами иных цивилизаций. Новый телескоп Веры Рубин поможет проверить, нет ли среди них рукотворных объектов. Возможно, первый контакт уже на пороге.

title: How Information Gives Birth to Particles from Nothing
oneliner: Stirring invisible quantum threads in the void turns information into real particles.
description: Physicists have derived formulas proving: when quantum ties are broken, the vacuum responds by birthing matter. The calculations hold for acceleration, black holes, and radioactive decay. Information is no longer a shadow of reality — it's its architect.
abstract: For the first time, a quantitative link has been established between entanglement entropy (a measure of quantum correlations) and particle creation. The authors derived formulas predicting the radiation spectrum, total number, and energy of particles for accelerated motion, black hole evaporation, and beta decay. It turns out that even in calm, nearly classical conditions, a weak flow of information can create matter — much like slow-melting ice feeds a river. This work turns the metaphor 'it from bit' into measurable physical reality.
threads: Пустое пространство не так пусто, как кажется. Из-за квантовой запутанности вакуум похож на сеть невидимых связей. Если её встряхнуть — ускорением или гравитацией чёрной дыры — связи рождают реальные частицы. Теперь физики точно рассчитали, как информация превращается в материю: мир буквально соткан из данных.

title: How Twisted Light Reveals a Black Hole's Charge
oneliner: A new method uses the rotation of light beams to measure the electric charge of the supermassive black hole M87*.
description: Light bending around a black hole gets twisted — by the spiraling of the beams you can measure its electric charge. Astrophysicists applied this approach to images of the M87 galaxy and obtained an upper limit for the first time: the hole is almost neutral. The method is universal and suitable for h
abstract: The rotating spacetime around black holes behaves like a non-uniform lens, twisting the light that passes through. By measuring this twist — the orbital angular momentum of the radiation — we can estimate the black hole's electric charge. Applying this approach to M87* observations yielded an upper limit on the charge-to-mass ratio: no more than 0.39. Although astrophysical black holes are expected to be neutral, the technique can be applied to any type of 'charge' that defines different solutions to gravitational equations. Thus, the orbital angular momentum of light becomes a new tool for independently testing gravity theories in strong fields, complementing traditional shadow observations.
threads: Свет, проходя мимо чёрной дыры, закручивается, как вода вокруг слива. По силе этого вращения астрономы впервые измерили электрический заряд дыры-гиганта в сердце галактики M87 — почти ноль. Но метод универсален: так можно будет искать даже неуловимые новые поля.

title: Melody of Disorder: What a Black Hole's Light Says About Chaos
oneliner: The light of a black hole, broken down into frequencies, reveals chaos through noise rather than a pure note.
description: Gas clumps around black holes move either in predictable orbits or chaotically. Scientists have found a way to distinguish them by analyzing the frequency spectrum of light. Chaotic orbits produce a blurred signal, like noise, while regular ones produce sharp peaks, like a pure note. This method wil
abstract: In astrophysics, chaos is predicted by theory but rarely observed due to the lack of reliable detection methods. A new study numerically models light curves (brightness changes) of hot spots moving in curved Kerr spacetime under an external uniform magnetic field. It turns out the power spectra of these curves differ dramatically: regular orbits produce isolated sharp peaks, while chaotic ones yield broad, smeared peaks of low amplitude. It's like the difference between the ring of a tuning fork and the rumble of the surf. This approach could turn light curves into a tool for spotting chaos in strong gravitational fields.
threads: Вокруг чёрных дыр газовые сгустки могут кружиться как часы, а могут метаться в хаосе. Учёные догадались: если превратить их свет в «звук», то порядок звучит чистой нотой, а хаос — шумом ветра. Такой приём откроет невидимые ритмы гравитационных монстров.

title: Scandium-45: The Key to Clocks More Precise Than Atomic Ones
oneliner: The 'ticking' of a scandium-45 nucleus is 10 billion times more precise than the best atomic clocks.
description: Physicists excited scandium-45 nuclei with an X-ray laser and saw that in a solid crystal, vibrations hinder ideal precision. But the transition's natural purity promises nuclear clocks with an error of less than a second over the age of the universe. This opens the door to ultra-precise measurement
abstract: The transition in the scandium-45 nucleus has a record-narrow natural width of 1.4 femtoelectronvolts and a quality factor of ~10^19, outperforming atomic clocks. Experiments at the European X-ray laser confirmed the long lifetime of the isomer and revealed a new channel of elastic fluorescence with an internal conversion coefficient of about 390. However, the nuclear scattering signal vanished 2 ms after excitation — the solid broadens the resonance hundreds of times. It's like trying to hear a whisper over the clangor of a forge.
threads: Внутри ядра скандия-45 происходит почти идеально ровное «тиканье» — как маятник, качающийся без помех. Учёные заглянули в этот процесс и обнаружили, что в твёрдом материале вибрации немного смазывают ход часов. Это открытие может привести к созданию ядерных часов, которые не собьются даже за миллиарды лет.

title: Fullerene: A Ball That Shoots Single Photons
oneliner: Carbon-60 molecules embedded in ordinary plastic turn it into a reliable source of single photons at room temperature.
description: Cheap fullerenes added to plastic emit light strictly in single-photon packets — fast and fail-proof. This simple material opens the door to affordable quantum devices for secure communication and computing.
abstract: Researchers have shown that ordinary C60 fullerenes (carbon molecules shaped like a soccer ball), embedded in polystyrene, can emit single photons on demand at room temperature. These emitters have a short fluorescence lifetime, ensuring a high emission rate. Using widely available, low-cost materials and simple fabrication offers a reliable and scalable alternative to existing expensive single-photon sources. This breakthrough could democratize quantum photonic technologies.
threads: Представьте кран, из которого капает не вода, а свет строго по одному фотону. Фуллерен — углеродный шарик в простом пластике — делает это при комнатной температуре. Дёшево и сердито: квантовая связь выходит из лабораторий в быт.

title: Starquakes: The Birth of Cosmic Radio Signals
oneliner: For the first time, simulations show how the shaking of a magnetar's crust gives rise to powerful bursts of radio emission.
description: Magnetars are dead stars with colossal magnetic fields. Their crust can crack, sending a pulse into space that can outshine an entire galaxy. New calculations combine seismology and electrodynamics, tracing the full chain from the jolt to a fast radio burst.
abstract: For the first time, simulations of elastic motions of the magnetar crust and relativistic electrodynamics of its magnetosphere have been combined. It is shown that surface oscillations launch fast magnetosonic and Alfvén waves (magnetic field oscillations), which transition into a nonlinear regime, creating shock waves, relativistic explosions, and current sheets. The magnetosphere partially 'combs out', resembling a strongly disturbed split-monopole configuration. These results impose important constraints on fast radio burst mechanisms, especially for frequently repeating sources, and point to possible quasi-periodic signatures of crustal oscillations in magnetospheric waves.
threads: Мёртвая звезда размером с город вдруг вздрагивает — и её магнитное поле идёт рябью, как плёнка на барабане. Колебания превращаются в пучки частиц, мчащихся почти со скоростью света. Мы ловим их как загадочные радиовсплески из далёкого космоса. Учёные впервые проследили весь путь — от трещины в коре до вспышки.

title: White Dwarf Plays Hide-and-Seek with Cosmic Dust
oneliner: A dead star hides behind dust clouds every five hours — and these clouds can vanish without a trace for months.
description: The white dwarf ZTF J1944+4557 plays hide-and-seek: its light dims by 30% every 4 hours 58 minutes. The dust particles here are no smaller than 0.2 microns and they dim all colors of the spectrum equally. But the strangest twist: the pattern of eclipses changes faster than the orbit, and sometimes a
abstract: Deep (over 30%) irregular eclipses with a period of 4.97 hours have been detected around the white dwarf ZTF J1944+4557 — near the Roche limit, where tidal forces tear bodies apart. This is only the second known system with debris transits after WD 1145+017. Spectra revealed circumstellar gas, and the lack of color dependence points to large dust grains (larger than 0.2 microns). The transits completely vanished for seven months and then resumed, as if someone restarted a cosmic pendulum. This system is a natural laboratory for studying the depletion and replenishment of disks from destroyed planets.
threads: Белый карлик, мёртвая звезда размером с Землю, каждые 5 часов ныряет в облака собственной пыли, а потом вдруг снова становится чистым. Эта космическая игра в прятки помогает понять, как умирают планеты.

title: How a baby planet drinks gas: a new discovery
oneliner: For the first time, we've seen a forming planet greedily sucking in gas from a cosmic cloud.
description: A baby planet absorbing gas has been discovered around the star AB Aurigae. Light from hydrogen revealed its growth with a unique pattern—astronomers observed this for the first time in a planet embryo. This is direct evidence of how giants like Jupiter grow.
abstract: Using MUSE on the VLT, astronomers studied the protoplanet AB Aur b in the Hα line and captured a unique spectrum: short-wavelength emission (shifted by -100 km/s) and long-wavelength absorption (+75 km/s). This resembles an inverse P Cygni profile — a signature of infalling cold gas, typical of accreting stars. AB Aur b is the second protoplanetary system detected in Hα, and the first with such a profile, like a 'whirlpool' in the spectrum. The nature of the signal is still unclear: it could be accretion, reflected light, or something else.
threads: Астрономы застали экзопланету-младенца за «кормлением»: газ со скоростью 100 км/с падает на неё, создавая уникальный световой узор. Открытие у звезды AB Возничего — первое прямое указание на такой процесс у зародыша планеты. Это как увидеть, как строится газовый гигант вроде Юпитера.

title: Moon Bell: A New Way to Catch Gravitational Waves
oneliner: Moon Bell: How the stillness of our satellite will allow us to hear the gravitational hum of the Universe.
description: Scientists from the LILA mission aim to transform the Moon into a gravitational wave detector. Devoid of noise, the Moon will ring like a bell when hit by waves, and laser sensors will measure these vibrations. This opens the low-frequency band and lets us study black hole mergers right back to the
abstract: In the millihertz range, gravitational waves cannot be detected on Earth due to seismic background noise. The Moon, however, is seismically quiet, and its surface vibrates at its natural frequencies (normal modes) under the influence of waves, like a giant resonator. The LILA project will deploy a laser interferometer on the Moon, whose sensitivity in the Pioneer phase will be limited only by Brownian thermal noise of the optics — enough to study black holes and neutron stars. The next stage, LILA Horizon, promises to reach the horizon of the Universe. The Moon will become an instrument capable of hearing the hum of cosmic birth.
threads: Луна – самый тихий уголок Солнечной системы. На ней нет землетрясений, океанов и ветра. Теперь физики поняли, как превратить эту тишину в гигантский детектор гравитационных волн, способный услышать слияния черных дыр с самого края Вселенной. 🚀🌖

title: Invisible Black Holes from Magnetic Fields
oneliner: Magnetic fields of the early Universe explain both dark matter and future gravitational waves.
description: Scientists have found a mechanism for the birth of primordial black holes from magnetic vortices in the early Universe. These invisible objects may make up dark matter, and their formation created gravitational waves detectable by future detectors.
abstract: To explain dark matter, primordial black holes are often considered, but their formation typically requires fine-tuning of parameters. A new study proposes an elegant mechanism: in a magnetogenesis scenario (the birth of magnetic fields in the early Universe), conditions naturally arise for the birth of black holes with just the right masses that do not evaporate by our time. Strikingly, such a scenario not only provides all the dark matter (for reheating temperatures of 100–300 thousand GeV) but also predicts a gravitational-wave background that future detectors like LISA and SKA will be able to register. It's like an echo from the cosmic kitchen where the fabric of the universe was cooked up.
threads: В ранней Вселенной бушевали магнитные бури. Они не только закрутили космический магнетизм, но и создали невидимые чёрные дыры. Эти дыры могут быть тёмной материей, а их рождение — породить гравитационные волны, которые скоро уловят детекторы.

title: An Invisible Intruder Smashed Into the Milky Way: The Impact Scar Found in Stars
oneliner: A stellar 'dent' has been discovered in the Milky Way — a telltale mark left by a dark galaxy invisible to telescopes.
description: In the Milky Way's stellar disk, astronomers spotted a region where hundreds of stars suddenly changed their speed. Nearby, gas clouds are crumpled into a giant 'finger,' and at its tip, there's not a single star. It's a dwarf galaxy made of dark matter that rammed our disk millions of years ago, le
abstract: Sifting through data from the Gaia space telescope, scientists have spotted a local dip in the vertical speeds of stars across the Milky Way's disk. It sits right where interstellar gas is showing a whole set of disturbances—a gaping void, a molecular ring, and a towering vertical hydrogen 'fountain.' Most likely, this is the scar left by a dark subhalo (a clump of dark matter the size of a dwarf galaxy) that never gave birth to stars. It's rare, direct evidence for the existence of lightweight dark structures, giving us a crucial test for models of how galaxies form.
threads: Звёзды в Млечном Пути «проваливаются» вниз из-за пролёта невидимой карликовой галактики. Эта тёмная «неудачница» не зажгла ни одной звезды, но оставила след в движении звёзд. Такие находки объясняют, как устроена тёмная материя.

title: Clouds of Molten Rock on an Alien Planet
oneliner: The James Webb Space Telescope has detected clouds of molten rock for the first time in the atmosphere of a planet heated to stellar temperatures.
description: On the dayside of WASP-121b, where rocks vaporize from the heat, Webb’s telescope has spotted clouds of calcium titanate — the same white pigment used in paint. The discovery unveils the alchemy of ultra-hot worlds and how the host star gradually destroys its planet.
abstract: Peering with the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers have dissected the dayside glow of ultra-hot Jupiter WASP-121b. Not only did they firmly spot water, CO, SiO, TiO, and VO, but for the first time in an exoplanet’s atmosphere, they found clouds of calcium titanate—a stubborn mineral that likely grabs titanium, which is why it’s scarce in gas form. The chemistry check also showed a carbon-to-oxygen ratio of 0.96 and a metallicity around 5 times the Sun’s. This world now serves as a crucial litmus test for atmospheric chemistry models.
threads: На планете WASP-121b, где даже горы испаряются, телескоп Уэбба впервые разглядел облака из минеральной пыли. Адская жара превращает камень в газ, а газ — в светящуюся дымку. Открытие обещает разгадать тайны рождения и гибели сверхгорячих миров.

title: Black Hole Yields: Weak Spot Found
oneliner: Physicists have found for the first time an influence that actually deforms a black hole — a field of matter particles.
description: Black holes are not always perfectly rigid. Under the influence of a field of matter particles (such as electrons), they bend, although it was previously thought that weak influences couldn't deform them. Ordinary fields, like light or gravity, leave them unperturbed, but matter does not.
abstract: We used to think black holes were immune to being stretched by static fields like light or gravity—their Love numbers were always zero. But now we've found a crack in that armor: fermionic fields (the stuff of matter) can leave a mark, with non-zero Love numbers for a spinning Kerr black hole, and we've even got an exact formula. Oddly, the dissipative part vanishes for static disturbances, so there's no superradiance. This splits the boson and fermion realms, pointing to a breaking of hidden symmetries.
threads: Чёрные дыры оказались не такими уж каменными: впервые найдено воздействие, которое их деформирует — фермионное поле. Раньше считалось, что они непробиваемы для слабых полей, но материя всё-таки оставляет вмятину. Нарушение скрытых симметрий гравитации.

title: How Symmetry Saves Quantum Entanglement from Heat
oneliner: Usually high temperature breaks quantum bonds, but symmetry holds them even in scorching systems.
description: Physicists have discovered: if a quantum system obeys a strict symmetry rule—like charge conservation—its particles remain entangled even at infinitely high temperature. This finding explains how order survived the Big Bang and promises quantum devices that don’t fear overheating.
abstract: Usually, quantum systems lose entanglement when heated — "sudden death" sets in. But scientists have discovered that symmetry can prevent this. For systems with Abelian symmetry (like a conserved charge), thermal states retain non-zero entanglement negativity at any temperature. A similar effect is confirmed for fermions: their entanglement "survives" the heat, proving a number of old hypotheses. In other words, global order shields quantum correlations from thermal chaos — like a building's frame holds the walls during a tremor.
threads: Квантовая запутанность — как танец невидимых партнёров. Обычно жара разрывает их связь, но новое исследование показало: если партнёры обязаны держаться за руки (симметрия), они не расстаются никогда. Это путь к квантовым устройствам, устойчивым к нагреву.

title: Who’s Who in the World of Particles and Antiparticles
oneliner: A new algorithm identifies B mesons and their antipodes by the full decay picture, not by individual details, boosting accuracy by 35%.
description: Physicists at LHCb have learned to identify neutral B mesons—particles that can turn into their antiparticles—more precisely. The new method analyzes not individual traits but the entire decay picture, improving accuracy by 35%. This brings us closer to understanding why matter defeated antimatter i
abstract: Physicists at LHCb have developed an algorithm that identifies the flavor (type) of neutral B mesons at the moment of their birth with high accuracy. Unlike previous methods, it analyzes all particles produced during hadronization (the process of 'dressing' quarks into hadrons), not just the decay products. Well-studied decays B0→J/ψ K+π- and B0_s→D_s-π+ were used for calibration. As a result, identification efficiency increased by 35% for B0 and by 20% for B0_s. It's like learning to guess exactly which hammer was used just by observing all the splashes from the impact.
threads: При столкновении протонов рождаются близнецы: частица и её антимир. Раньше их различали, словно узнавая человека только по носу. Теперь, используя весь «рисунок» траекторий, физики улучшили распознавание на 35%. Маленький шаг к разгадке великой тайны антивещества.

title: Einstein's Paradox: Why the Quantum World Can't Be Fooled
oneliner: The old debate about bypassing quantum uncertainty is finally settled.
description: In 1935, Einstein devised a thought experiment that seemed to allow knowing both the exact position and speed of a particle at once. New work proves: any measurement shatters this illusion, and there's no way around the fundamental prohibition.
abstract: The Einstein–Podolsky–Rosen (EPR) paradox suggested that you could outsmart the Heisenberg uncertainty principle: first, find out a particle's momentum without measuring it, then measure its position, getting precise values for both. New research proves there's no contradiction, thanks to two equivalent methods — quantum conditional expectation and von Neumann's post-measurement state. It turns out that the post-measurement prediction is described by an operator-valued function of the observables, which automatically preserves uncertainty. No matter how you mix and match actions, quantum nature won't let you know both the position and momentum of a particle exactly at the same time.
threads: Эйнштейн считал, что можно обмануть квантовую неопределённость. В 1935 году он придумал опыт, где две частицы раскрывают друг друга. Казалось, вот-вот падёт принцип Гейзенберга. Но свежий разбор доказал: измерение разрушает предсказание. Природа хитрее — никаких лазеек, квантовый мир стоит насмерть.

title: Red Dots Are Not Twins of Dusty Quasars
oneliner: Little red galaxies discovered by the James Webb telescope turned out to be nothing like bright dusty quasars.
description: The Webb telescope spotted a multitude of tiny red galaxies in the early Universe. They were confused with dazzlingly bright galaxies where a black hole heats dust like a blast furnace. Though their color 'portraits' are similar, the red dots have cooler dust—meaning their 'engines' run more modestl
abstract: «Little Red Dots» (LRDs) are compact dusty galaxies with a V-shaped spectrum, discovered by JWST. They resemble «blue-excess dusty quasars» (BHDs)—rare objects accreting near the limit onto a black hole. But detailed spectral analysis showed: LRDs contain less dust, their infrared emission is bluer, indicating no hot dust from accretion, and the blue excess is not scattered light from the nucleus. Unlike BHDs, which are born in galaxy collisions, LRDs are compact and rarely detected in X-rays, suggesting a different evolutionary path.
threads: Крошечные красные точки, заполонившие снимки «Джеймса Уэбба», оказались не теми, кем казались. У них нет горячей пыли от прожорливых чёрных дыр — значит, в ранней Вселенной работал совсем другой механизм роста галактик. Это меняет картину первых миллиардов лет после Большого взрыва.

title: The Master Key to the Secrets of Spinning Black Holes
oneliner: Scientists found a way to replace mountains of equations with a single action — taking a derivative of a generating function.
description: Spinning black holes and neutron stars: their calculations used to be incredibly cumbersome. Scientists proposed a method where everything reduces to a generating function. It's like a universal dough — from it, by simple differentiation, you get any characteristic of the system. The new approach ha
abstract: Using effective heavy-particle theory (a streamlined method for complex interactions), physicists built Kerr generating functions—a tool that describes scattering of any object off a rotating black hole to any precision. The core calculations boil down to simple differentiation with respect to spin, like a universal key to a cipher. As a case study, they explored tidal deformations of a neutron star in a Kerr black hole field, obtaining compact four-loop results (accuracy on the order of G⁵) for the first time.
threads: Физики нашли математический трюк: одна «производящая функция» заменяет горы вычислений у вращающейся чёрной дыры. Теперь сложнейший расчёт — это просто производная. Удалось детально увидеть, как чёрная дыра разрывает нейтронную звезду при встрече.

title: A Bond Beyond Time
oneliner: A muon experiment proved their connection ignores whether measurement or decay came first.
description: Muon pairs are born with strictly linked properties. It turns out the link holds even if one particle disintegrates before the other is measured. Our observations aren't a snapshot of reality, just a handy tool.
abstract: The study shows that for entangled muon pairs, correlations between spin and decay products do not depend on what happened first — measurement or decay. This suggests that quantum entanglement does not obey the familiar arrow of time. Interestingly, the same data can be interpreted differently in different frames of reference. The findings strengthen the view that the Copenhagen interpretation is merely a mathematical tool, not physical reality.
threads: Квантовая сцепка не знает, что такое «раньше» или «позже». Эксперимент с мюонами показал: взаимосвязь частиц сохраняется, даже если одна уже распалась. Для корреляций временной порядок — как цвет галстука у близнецов: не важно, кто первый его наденет, всё равно они одинаковы. Это наносит удар по привычной картине времени как потока событий.

title: Carbon Giant by a Dead Star
oneliner: JWST spotted a soot planet orbiting a pulsar: its carbon-rich atmosphere turns theories upside down.
description: A gas giant the size of Jupiter orbits the pulsar PSR J2322-2650b, heated to 1900°C. Its atmosphere is thick soot of carbon chains, not the usual water vapor. Carbon is a hundred times more abundant than oxygen compared to normal, and ten thousand times more than nitrogen. This doesn't fit the 'blac
abstract: Astronomers used JWST to observe PSR J2322-2650b, a Jupiter-mass object orbiting a pulsar. Its atmosphere, unlike planets around normal stars, is dominated by carbon molecules C2 and C3 under strong western winds. The carbon-to-oxygen (>100) and carbon-to-nitrogen (>10,000) ratios are extreme: the material resembles soot more than a gas mixture. This challenges "black widow" models, where companions were thought to be remnants of stellar cores with diverse compositions.
threads: Вокруг мёртвой звезды-пульсара вращается планета, чья атмосфера состоит из сажи и углеродных молекул. Это удивительно: обычно такие миры — остатки звёзд — должны содержать много кремния и кислорода. Загадка углеродного гиганта бросает вызов астрофизике.

title: Cosmic Rain Will Improve Hurricane Forecasts
oneliner: Streams of invisible particles from space allow more accurate measurements of atmospheric density and early warnings of cyclones.
description: An invisible stream of particles from space helps assess atmospheric density. Scientists have shown that even a small muon detector can significantly improve hurricane predictions. This method provides a three-dimensional picture unattainable with ordinary pressure sensors.
abstract: Weather models need accurate air density, but conventional sensors only sample sparsely. Atmospheric muons—showered down by cosmic rays—could change that because their flux at ground level reflects the density of the column above. In a simulated study with cyclone Freddy, assimilating muon data from a compact detector markedly improved the estimated atmospheric state. Remarkably, this boost was not seen when adding extra pressure sensors, suggesting muons capture something truly distinct. Like a natural X-ray, muons scan the atmosphere's mass.
threads: Каждую секунду сквозь вас пролетают сотни невидимых стрел из космоса — мюонов. Их количество меняется от плотности воздуха. Учёные поняли, как с помощью этих частиц заранее узнавать о зарождающихся ураганах — и теперь даже маленький детектор улучшит прогнозы. Космос становится нашим метеорологом.

title: Neutrino Travelers from the Fifth Dimension
oneliner: Right-handed neutrinos from a micro-dimension leave sharp steps in electron energy — and the KATRIN experiment is ready to catch them.
description: Physicists have proposed a way to test the hypothesis of a fifth dimension the size of a bacterium. If right-handed neutrinos live in it, then by measuring the energy of electrons in KATRIN, we will see sharp steps — one or more. Their number and position will reveal the properties of the hidden wor
abstract: Right-handed neutrinos might not be ordinary particles but 'inhabitants' of an extra microscopic dimension (about a micron in size), as suggested by the dark dimension hypothesis. The KATRIN experiment, analyzing tritium beta decay, can detect such neutrinos through distinctive kinks in the electron spectrum — their position depends on the particle's mass. If the neutrino's bulk mass is small, a series of kinks from Kaluza-Klein excitations appears (like the overtones of a string); if large, a single kink emerges. Calculations show that most of the parameter space, including the compactification scale and Yukawa coupling, lies within KATRIN's reach.
threads: В крошечных складках пространства могут прятаться нейтрино-невидимки. Каждое такое нейтрино рождает целую семью более тяжёлых двойников. Эксперимент KATRIN способен заметить их по резким изломам в энергии электронов. Если сигнал найдут, это станет окном в дополнительное измерение.

title: Spring Crystal: Giant Response Without Coils
oneliner: A bulk crystal produces inductance without wire coils or superconductors.
description: Crystal Mn3Si2Te6, exposed to a magnetic field and a weak current, pushes back like a spring refusing to let the current change. Inside, microscopic whirlpools of electron flow rearrange in sync. This could miniaturize components for quantum electronics without tricky fabrication.
abstract: In the magnetic material Mn3Si2Te6, colossal inductance (the ability to resist current change) at the millihenry level has been discovered. It's caused by the rearrangement of domains consisting of chiral orbital currents—tiny vortex-like electron movements flowing through the crystal lattice. This effect works under everyday conditions, without the need for superconductivity or nanostructures, and acts like a built-in 'inductance coil' inside a plain crystal. It shatters classical notions and opens doors to next-gen orbital electronics.
threads: Обнаружен кристалл, который превращается в гигантскую «электрическую пружину». Магнитное поле и слабый ток запускают в нём вихри электронных потоков, которые не хотят останавливаться и выдают мощный всплеск напряжения. Никаких катушек и сверхпроводников — такая встроенная индуктивность может сделать квантовые схемы намного компактнее.

title: How Acceleration Turns Vacuum into a Hot Bath
oneliner: The Unruh effect: an accelerating observer feels warmth from empty space.
description: A particle thermometer accelerated through vacuum heats up, and the way it reaches equilibrium depends on the field type and dimensions. Intriguingly, it heats faster than it cools, mimicking the Mpemba effect. This asymmetry provides a fingerprint to identify the Unruh effect.
abstract: Here’s the deal: the Unruh effect says that if you accelerate through a vacuum, it stops feeling empty—instead, you detect heat. In a new study, researchers used an Unruh-DeWitt detector (basically a model particle) to see how this quantum 'heating' pushes the detector toward equilibrium. Guess what? The path this process traces on the Bloch sphere (a way to picture the state) depends on the kinds of fields and the number of spacetime dimensions. They found a quantum version of the Mpemba effect: heating goes faster than cooling when you track how 'close' states get (called fidelity). What’s more, the peak difference in closeness can tell apart Unruh thermalization from regular heating in a thermal bath.
threads: Пустой космос вовсе не пуст: если разогнаться до невообразимой скорости, вакуум нагреется. Физики выяснили, что такой нагрев идёт быстрее остывания — квантовый вариант эффекта Мпембы. Этот парадокс поможет построить детектор, который отличит тепло ускорения от обычного теплового потока.

title: Why is the Moon so similar to Earth?
oneliner: A new theory explains the Moon's chemical resemblance to Earth: in an ocean of molten rock, dense rocks sank and lighter ones rose.
description: According to the leading hypothesis, the Moon was born from a collision between Earth and a planet called Theia. However, calculations predicted the Moon should consist mostly of Theia’s material, yet it’s almost a chemical twin of Earth. A new model cracks the paradox: if Theia’s interior was dense
abstract: According to the giant-impact hypothesis, the Moon formed from debris after Theia collided with proto-Earth. But models show that over 40% of the disk was Theia material, while the Moon’s isotopic composition is nearly identical to Earth’s—this puzzle is called the lunar isotopic crisis. The authors suggest that Theia’s mantle was iron-rich, and as the layered magma ocean (molten layer) solidified, an upper crust formed from proto-Earth material, concealing Theia’s inner substance. Thus, the lunar surface naturally became isotopically similar to Earth.
threads: Луна — почти близнец Земли по изотопам, хотя возникла из обломков чужой планеты. Загадка решается просто: при застывании лунного океана магмы тяжёлые породы Тейи утонули, а лёгкие земные всплыли и затвердели сверху. Так что наш спутник — лишь притворщик с инопланетным нутром.

title: Seven Stars from One Dough
oneliner: Astronomers have for the first time seen seven baby stars born from a single disk, just like from a batch of dough.
description: In the nebula NGC 6334I, a system of seven protostars was discovered in a common gas disk. The disk, like fragile dough, tore apart under its own weight into pieces, and each condensed into a star. Previously, such a mechanism was thought to be common only for binary stars. The discovery proves that
abstract: Astronomers have observationally confirmed for the first time that a system of seven protostars can form through fragmentation of a gas-dust disk. In the NGC 6334IN region, a disk with seven stellar embryos was found at distances of 181–461 AU (like from the Sun to Neptune). Gravitational instability of the disk led to its breakup into separate clumps—a mechanism previously only hypothetical for such high-multiplicity systems. The discovery sheds light on the birth of large stellar families in dense clusters.
threads: Газовый диск разрывается, как тесто для пиццы, и из каждого куска рождается звезда. Впервые засняли процесс с семью «близнецами» одновременно. Это переписывает учебники по рождению звёзд: раньше думали, что так появляются лишь пары, а оказалось — можно сразу семерых.

title: The Higgs Field Opens a Passage Through a Black Hole
oneliner: In extreme gravity, the Higgs field gives birth to antigravity, turning a black hole into a traversable region.
description: Usually passive, the Higgs field inside a black hole gives rise to antigravity, preventing collapse into a point and creating a tunnel. The i(SM+GR) theory unites particle physics and gravity, predicting traversable black holes and the possibility of a universe before the Big Bang.
abstract: In strong gravity, the Higgs field behaves non-perturbatively: instead of small fluctuations, it actively changes the geometry of spacetime, generating antigravity. This makes singularities traversable and ensures geodesic completeness (no "edge" to spacetime). This behavior is built into a modified theory i(SM+GR) with local conformal (scale) symmetry. The model not only preserves low-energy successes but also predicts effects inside black holes and before the Big Bang, offering a fresh perspective on the information loss problem.
threads: Внутри чёрной дыры поле Хиггса сходит с ума: вместо того, чтобы просто давать частицам массу, оно генерирует антигравитацию и размыкает сингулярность, превращая её в портал. Возможно, так можно путешествовать сквозь чёрные дыры и увидеть, что было до Большого взрыва.

title: How Acceleration Heats Up the Void
oneliner: Rapid acceleration makes empty space radiate heat—physicists have found a way to spot it.
description: Physicists have simulated the radiation that appears when electrons are suddenly accelerated by a laser. This 'heat from nothing,' predicted by the Unruh effect, was teased out from other signals using ultra-precise simulations and extremely small observation angles. The finding brings us closer to
abstract: The Unruh effect (the prediction that an accelerating observer perceives the vacuum as thermal radiation) has yet to be detected. The study simulates realistic laser-electron collisions, similar to those planned at FACET-II and LUXE. Unruh radiation was calculated as scattering of thermal photons, with nonlinear Compton radiation serving as background. Narrow spectral-angular windows were found where the Unruh signal peaks: for LUXE, the signal-to-background ratio jumps a hundredfold. This is like picking out a whisper from the noise if you know the right angle and frequency.
threads: Пустое пространство не такое холодное, как кажется: если разогнаться почти до скорости света, вакуум засияет тепловым излучением. Физики рассчитали, как поймать этот призрачный свет в лазерных экспериментах: возможно, уже в ближайшие годы мы увидим «тепло от ничто».

title: Time from Disorder: How Atoms Revealed an Internal Clock
oneliner: Physicists have shown: time is measured not by ticking, but by the growth of disorder inside a system.
description: Scientists split a cloud of atoms and, by tracking rising disorder, ordered events in one half without seeing the other. This confirms that time emerges from internal changes. Such an approach will help us understand how time flows in black holes and right after the Big Bang.
abstract: In an experiment with an ultracold Bose condensate (a state of matter at extremely low temperatures), the trap was split by an optical barrier into an observed and an unobserved part. Inspired by ideas from quantum gravity, where time can disappear, an "entropic time" was built from coarse-grained entropy (a measure of disorder) and shown to order events in the observed part during repeated expansions and contractions of the cloud. Moreover, they derived a Schrödinger equation that operates with this internal time and reproduces the system's evolution. This is the first rigorous laboratory test of relational models of time, where time is not imposed from outside but emerges from internal processes—much like the plot of a movie isn't determined by a stopwatch but by the sequence of scenes.
threads: Что, если время — не тиканье незримых часов, а ощущение, рождающееся из перемен? Физики взяли облачко атомов, разделили лазером надвое и заставили одну половину жить по внутреннему распорядку. Оказалось, без внешнего секундомера события чётко упорядочиваются — достаточно следить за ростом беспорядка. Такой «энтропийный» подход объясняет, как течёт время в чёрных дырах и сразу после Большого взрыва, где привычным часам не на что опереться.

title: Cosmic Necklace Reveals Secrets of Galaxy Birth
oneliner: Three colliding galaxies ejected gas that curled into glowing beads on an invisible thread — this is how a cosmic giant is born.
description: Astronomers peered into the early Universe and saw how three massive galaxies, merging, eject gas. This gas compresses into clumps resembling necklace beads and glows brightly. These cosmic 'jewelry' pieces are an important stage in the construction of the most massive galaxies.
abstract: Observations of the protocluster SPT2349-56 (12.8 billion light-years away) by the ALMA telescope showed that when massive galaxies merge, gas is ejected in the form of tidal tails, which break up into clumps — like beads on a string. Their density is comparable to the spiral arms of nearby galaxies, and the bright glow in the ionized carbon [CII] line indicates shock heating. Such tidal ejections could be the most important mechanism in the rapid assembly of giant galaxies in the early universe.
threads: В далёком космосе три огромные галактики врезаются друг в друга, как мегаполисы, сливающиеся в один. От ударов разлетаются светящиеся газовые «бусины», нанизанные на невидимую нить. Это буйство рождения звёзд и, возможно, способ, которым возникли самые массивные галактики во Вселенной.

title: Black hole merger confirms Hawking's area law
oneliner: The total horizon area of two merging black holes became larger — exactly as Hawking's law predicts.
description: A clear signal from the merger of two black holes, each about 33 solar masses, confirmed Hawking's theorem: the final horizon area turned out larger than the sum of the initial ones. This strengthens the link between black hole area and entropy.
abstract: Signal GW250114, detected by LIGO, came from the merger of two nearly identical black holes (about 33 solar masses each) with minimal spin. After the merger, the oscillations of the new hole matched the Kerr model perfectly, and their frequencies differed from theory by no more than 30% — confirming the nature of the remnant. Hawking's law was also tested: the horizon area grew, not shrank, as required by theory. Analogy: two droplets merge into one, and the total surface area does not decrease — a fundamental property of black holes.
threads: Две чёрные дыры весом по 33 Солнца слились в одну. Их общая «поверхность» — горизонт событий — стала больше, как и требует закон Хокинга. Площадь чёрных дыр только растёт, подобно энтропии. Возможно, это ключ к стреле времени.

title: Topological Physics at Room Temperature Using Light
oneliner: Physicists have realized the Haldane model without ultra-low temperatures for the first time, thanks to a laser lattice.
description: Exotic quantum states typically exist only in the chill of liquid helium. But here, scientists replaced cooling with a light lattice, where atoms move in unison like an orchestra. The collective glow drowns out random noise, making it impossible for chaos to break through. They not only reproduced t
abstract: The Haldane model—a theoretical cornerstone of topological insulators—had only been experimentally realized at liquid helium temperatures. Researchers have now achieved it at room temperature for the first time using atoms in superradiant lattices (collective synchronized emission in an ordered structure). The topological transition was detected through the difference in superradiance intensity between two Dicke states. The thermal resilience of the platform enabled deep modulation of parameters and yielded phases with high Chern numbers—a measure of the wavefunction's 'winding' that goes beyond the classical model.
threads: Обычно экзотические квантовые состояния требуют холода ниже, чем в космосе. Но физики обманули тепловой шум, заставив атомы петь в унисон в луче лазера. Теперь неуловимые топологические фазы живут при комнатной температуре — и обещают квантовые устройства без криостатов.

title: Quark Captivity: The Secret of the Invisible String's Snap
oneliner: A computer simulation reveals how the bonds holding quarks break, and the unexpected twists that come with it.
description: Using a simulation, scientists studied how the force strings connecting quarks break. The rupture turned out to be more complex: entire families of particles are born, and in a dense environment the process slows down. This discovery will help understand the microworld and can be tested on special d
abstract: Physicists simulated the dynamics of gluonic strings in non-Abelian SU(2) gauge theory with dynamical matter (2+1 dimensions). In the strong coupling and resonance regime, string breaking triggers a sharp Casimir energy plunge and the birth of mesons and baryon-antibaryon pairs — a hallmark of non-Abelian physics. At finite baryon density, a "baryon blockade" effect slows down the breaking. Away from resonance, magnetic interactions spawn gluonic loops and self-intersecting strings. These findings pave the way for future quantum simulators.
threads: Кварки, замкнутые в протоне, соединены невидимой струной, как бусинки на резинке. Когда струна лопается, рождаются сразу несколько новых частиц. Это открытие приближает нас к управлению микромиром.

title: Music from Quantum Randomness
oneliner: Scientists turned quantum randomness into live music.
description: By measuring the random properties of entangled light particles, scientists and artists create an audiovisual show that nature itself prevents from ever repeating.
abstract: Entangled photons are like a pair of dice that always land on the same number, even when rolled on opposite sides of the universe. In this performance, real-time results from a Bell test (a check for quantum correlations) are turned into sound and video. The result is a unique audiovisual piece that would be impossible without quantum physics. This fusion of science and art not only amazes but also expands our ideas of how to create using nature's fundamental laws.
threads: В одном зале свет рождает запутанные фотоны. Их танец, случайный, но связанный, управляет звуком и видео на сцене. Музыка, которую нельзя повторить — её дирижёром становится сама квантовая природа.

title: Axions Connect Two Cosmic Radio Mysteries
oneliner: Axions turning into radio waves explain the excess cosmic radio noise and the mysterious dip in the hydrogen signal.
description: Two cosmic mysteries—the excess of radio waves in the sky (ARCADE2) and the anomalously weak signal from the earliest hydrogen (EDGES)—have received a common explanation. The culprit could be axions, ultralight dark matter particles. In primordial magnetic fields, axions turn into radio waves, creat
abstract: Axions, lightweight particles (candidates for dark matter), could have converted into photons in the presence of primordial magnetic fields (PMFs). In the early Universe, when the axion mass matched the effective mass of photons in plasma, a resonance occurred, generating excess radio emission in the MHz–GHz range, which simultaneously explains the ARCADE2 radio background and the EDGES anomalous hydrogen absorption. Simulations show that axions with masses ~10⁻¹⁴–10⁻¹² eV and nearly scale-invariant PMFs at nano-Gauss levels are consistent with observations, while accounting for heating from field dissipation refines the allowed parameters.
threads: Вселенная наполнена невидимым радиошумом, а древний сигнал водорода имеет странный провал. Возможно, виновник — аксионы: частицы-призраки, которые иногда превращаются в радиоволны, как если бы маятник раскачался от невидимых толчков. Это открытие может объяснить сразу две загадки и дать ключ к темной материи.

title: Binary Stars Build Dark Matter Molecules
oneliner: Two swirling stars gather dark matter into a giant cloud that resembles a molecule.
description: Simulations show that a cloud of dark matter around two stars takes the shape of a molecule. The elongated orbits of the stars knock particles out of the cloud, causing their trajectories to round out. This process could leave a noticeable imprint in the gravitational-wave background, helping to rev
abstract: Ultralight bosons (like axions) around binary stars can form gravitational 'molecules.' Simulations show that as the stars draw closer, they shed particles (ionize), and this process in the early stages affects the orbit more strongly than gravitational waves, creating a kink in their spectrum. Interestingly, the inner part of the molecule ionizes solely due to the orbit's ellipticity, gradually making it circular — much like a wobbly wheel that becomes rounder over time from friction.
threads: Две звезды танцуют, и между ними рождается невидимая гигантская молекула из тёмной материи. Когда их орбиты колеблются, это космическое творение сбрасывает частицы, как встряхнутый атом теряет электроны. Такой процесс может оставить уникальный отпечаток в ряби пространства-времени — ключ к разгадке тёмной материи.

title: Cosmic Clocks Diverge: A New Mystery of the Universe's Expansion
oneliner: Measuring the expansion rate using Cepheids yielded 71.1 (km/s)/Mpc — the conflict with other methods has only intensified.
description: A new study used only pulsating Cepheid stars to calculate the expansion rate of the Universe. The resulting value of 71.1 (km/s)/Mpc turned out to be lower than the previous 'gold standard' and even further from the Planck satellite data. This sharpens the 'Hubble tension' and suggests our knowledg
abstract: The Hubble constant (the expansion rate of the Universe) remains a subject of debate: different methods yield incompatible values. The study re-analyzes the "distance ladder" based solely on Cepheids and their brightness, with improved accounting for data selection and galaxy motions. It obtains an estimate of H0 = 71.1 ± 1.4 km/s/Mpc, which is lower than the recent SH0ES value and deepens the discrepancy with the cosmic microwave background. This shows that even part of the ladder data can challenge the standard cosmological model, if "cosmic currents" are properly accounted for.
threads: Скорость расширения Вселенной снова перемерили. Новое число — 71.1 км/с/Мпк — почти на 2 ниже прежнего «золотого стандарта». Расхождение с данными спутника «Планк» стало ещё значительнее. Это намекает на неполноту наших знаний о космосе.

title: Black Hole or Impostor: The Ring of the Final Plunge
oneliner: The gravitational 'ringing' of infalling matter gives away whether a black hole is genuine.
description: By analyzing the final 'plunge' of matter, scientists search for spectral signatures of impostors. The fakes produce a ringing: a comb of resonances at low frequencies and a sharp cutoff at high ones. Stacking signals from many events lets them hear even the quietest notes.
abstract: When a small object falls rapidly into a massive body, gravitational waves carry information about its nature. If this object is not a black hole but an imitator (for example, an exotic star), two characteristic features appear in the spectrum: a series of sharp peaks at certain frequencies, like a comb, and a sharp break above a critical frequency.
