oneliner: The invisible envelope of dark matter around the Milky Way turns out to be flattened and twisted — like a deflated ball, rotated crosswise to the disk.
description: Using the motions of millions of stars, astronomers have built a 3D map of the dark halo. Its shape is not a sphere, but an elongated, flattened cloud. Surprisingly, the halo's long axis is almost perpendicular to the stellar disk. This orientation hints at a possible 'flip' of the disk in the past
abstract: Scientists reconstructed the shape of the Milky Way's dark matter halo by tracking the motions of giant stars, using a new dynamical model. The halo turned out to be triaxial, with its long and middle axes lying in a plane perpendicular to the galactic disk—like a donut standing on edge. Probably, the Milky Way's disk used to be oriented the same way, but then it flipped due to minor mergers with dwarf galaxies. This explains both the observed plane of satellites and links the disk's evolution with the shape of the dark halo.
threads: Тёмное гало Млечного Пути сжато с боков, а его длинная ось стоит вертикально звёздному диску. Это говорит о том, что наш диск когда-то перевернулся под действием гравитации мелких галактик-спутников. Невидимая «линза» объясняет таинственную плоскость карликовых галактик.

title: Quantum Record: 120 Entangled Qubits
oneliner: 120 qubits are woven into a Schrödinger's cat-like state for the first time—a record quantum ensemble.
description: A record entangled state of 120 qubits helps test quantum computers. Scientists used a clever scheme with temporary 'disentanglement' and error catching to bypass the fragility of connections. The achieved accuracy of 56% is an important step toward reliable computing and testing fundamental theorie
abstract: Scientists have created a record-breaking entangled state of 120 superconducting qubits—the so-called Greenberger–Horne–Zeilinger (GHZ) state, also known as a 'cat state'. This is a tough challenge because quantum entanglement is extremely sensitive to noise. By using optimized circuit compilation, error detection, and temporal 'uncomputing', they achieved a fidelity of 0.56(3) with a 28% post-selection rate. Several methods were used to verify the result, confirming its reliability. Such experiments are like trying to get 120 pendulums to swing in perfect sync in the same room—but now they help calibrate quantum processors.
threads: Физики спутали 120 кубитов в подобие «кота Шрёдингера» — рекордный квантовый ансамбль. Представьте оркестр из 120 музыкантов, играющих в унисон: малейшая фальшь разрушает гармонию. Учёные научились ловить ошибки, сохраняя хрупкую квантовую связь. Это шаг к надёжным квантовым компьютерам будущего.

title: A Scorching Planet Grew Two Tails
oneliner: The James Webb Telescope caught a scorching planet turning into a comet for the first time: its atmosphere boils off, creating two giant helium tails.
description: Astronomers using the James Webb Space Telescope observed the exoplanet WASP-121b—a blazing gas giant circling perilously close to its star. It turns out the planet is losing its atmosphere: helium streams into space in two comet-like tails. One tail outruns the planet and plunges into the star, whi
abstract: By observing the ultra-hot Jupiter WASP-121b for the first time continuously over an entire orbit with the James Webb Space Telescope, scientists detected helium absorption nearly 60% of the time. This proved the existence of a powerful atmospheric outflow that splits into two tails: a leading tail (moving toward the star) and a trailing tail (pushed away by radiation). Shifts in spectral lines revealed that the gas remains dense over vast distances. These tails are comet-like, but their dynamics are more complex—current models can’t yet accurately describe the mass loss.
threads: Оказывается, планеты могут быть похожи на кометы! Телескоп Уэбба разглядел у раскалённого гиганта WASP-121b два газовых хвоста: один падает на звезду, другой улетает прочь. Открытие меняет представления о том, как звёзды испаряют свои миры.

title: Comets around an alien star reveal their sizes
oneliner: The TESS telescope recorded 24 comet transits in front of the star RZ Psc, revealing their sizes: 1 to 7 km.
description: A swarm of comets around RZ Psc briefly dimmed the star's light as they passed in front of it. The TESS telescope caught these dimmings, allowing astronomers to estimate the sizes of these icy bodies—between one and seven kilometers. The size distribution resembled the belt of icy boulders beyond Ne
abstract: Astronomers using the TESS telescope recorded 24 passages of comets (exocomets) in front of the young star RZ Psc. Analysis showed that their size distribution follows a broken power-law—exactly like the objects of the Kuiper Belt in our Solar System. This is only the second case where such a distribution has been measured outside the Sun. The obtained estimates (sizes 1–7 km) helped predict the results of the future EVE mission: it will be able to register hundreds of similar events and even distinguish the dust composition—for example, graphite or olivine—in dozens of comet tails. Such a 'comet rain' opens a window into the early stages of planet formation.
threads: В 500 световых годах от нас телескоп TESS поймал 24 скоротечных «затмения»: это кометы проносились перед звездой RZ Psc. Их размеры подчиняются тому же небесному закону, что и ледяные глыбы пояса Койпера: много крохотных, мало крупных. Если запустить телескоп EVE, можно будет не только считать такие кометы сотнями, но и определять, из чего они слеплены.

title: Can the Moon have its own moon?
oneliner: Research shows: moons can have their own moons, and some of them could be habitable.
description: Astronomers investigated the possibility of submoons—small moons orbiting other moons. It turns out Earth could host an asteroid-sized submoon, while giant planets might have entire Earth-like worlds. But for life to emerge, a submoon needs about half of Earth's mass, and at that very mass its orbit
abstract: Submoons — satellites of satellites — could be habitable. Scientists simulated their tidal evolution. Earth would only retain asteroid-mass submoons (~10^15 kg), while a massive one (~4.6·10^17 kg) would push the Moon away by 30%. For a warm Jupiter Kepler1625b, chances are better: a submoon at 10% lunar mass is stable if its moon is 68% farther away. Giant submoons (1.8 M⊕) are stable, but for life a mass of ~0.5 M⊕ is needed (tectonics, atmosphere), where stability drops. At around ~0.1 M⊕ — a minimum due to the balance of tides and the Roche limit. This "bottleneck" could make habitable submoons rare.
threads: Луна может иметь свои собственные маленькие луны. Гравитация позволяет астероиду кружить вокруг Луны миллиарды лет. Но если такая луна-малышка будет слишком большой, она раскачает орбиту и улетит. Учёные говорят, что вокруг тёплых юпитеров такие луны могут стать настоящим домом для жизни. ✨

title: How Birds See Earth's Magnetic Field
oneliner: In birds' retinas, a protein called cryptochrome converts light and magnetic fields into an electrical signal, letting them see direction.
description: Scientists have figured out how birds navigate by the magnetic field. In their eyes, a protein called cryptochrome, under blue light, spawns a pair of field-sensitive particles. Their behavior shifts with the field's direction, and the protein generates an electrical signal. The bird's brain turns i
abstract: Scientists took a quantum-mechanical dive into cryptochrome, a protein in birds' retinas—the heart of their magnetic compass. When it catches blue light, a radical pair pops up (two separated electrons with linked spins). Turns out, a tiny bit of spin-orbit coupling, long dismissed as irrelevant, creates an electric dipole moment that's exquisitely tuned to the geomagnetic field's direction. Even with thermal noise, the setup is a neat molecular sensor: rotate the magnetic field, and the charge distribution shifts—a signal the nervous system might read. Think of it like a radio, but for Earth's magnetic hum.
threads: Глаз птицы скрывает в себе компас – не иглу, а белок, превращающий магнитное поле в электричество. Открытие позволит создать крошечные датчики для навигации без GPS.

title: Dark Matter: An Invisible Web Explains Three Mysteries
oneliner: One dark matter model — three astronomical puzzles solved at once.
description: Simulations show that if dark matter could clump, its lumps would explain perturbations in the stellar stream GD-1, the mysterious object from the gravitational lens, and the origin of the Fornax 6 cluster.
abstract: Self-interacting dark matter—where particles can bump into each other—can create incredibly dense clumps. Models reveal that clumps weighing about a million Suns can at once explain the warped star stream GD-1, the mysterious dark lens JVAS B1938+666, and the birth of the star cluster Fornax 6 in a tiny dwarf galaxy. That's a hat trick, making a compelling case for this type of dark matter.
threads: Невидимые «комочки» в космосе влияют на движение звёзд. Модель, созданная для объяснения странностей в звёздном потоке GD-1, неожиданно подошла и для других космических загадок. Это значит, что тёмная материя может цепляться сама за себя, слипаясь в плотные сгустки.

title: Rhythms of Black Holes: 34 Pulsations in Gamma-Ray Bursts
oneliner: Astronomers found 34 rhythmic oscillations in the emission of distant gamma-ray bursts, resembling the beat of a cosmic heart.
description: In data from the Swift telescope, 34 gamma-ray bursts with a clear rhythm were discovered. These bursts are born when a dying star collapses into a black hole, and its superheated gas swirls into a rapidly rotating disk. The rhythmic pulsations reveal the dance of matter around the hole: perhaps thi
threads: Гамма-всплески, рождающиеся при коллапсе массивных звезд, иногда «поют» с четким ритмом, словно космический метроном. Обнаружено 34 новых пульсации в излучении, и это намекает на танцы плазмы в дисках вокруг черных дыр, где само пространство-время закручивается вихрем.

title: Neural Network by Ear: How to Capture a Particle's Energy
oneliner: A neural network built into the detector listens in on the collider's noise and measures particle energy with record accuracy.
description: At the Large Hadron Collider, noise hinders precise particle energy measurement. A neural network embedded in the detector picks out the right signals and honestly assesses its own uncertainty — boosting the reliability of physics discoveries.
abstract: To upgrade the ATLAS detector at the Large Hadron Collider, researchers optimized neural networks for energy reconstruction in calorimeters under high pile-up conditions (multiple simultaneous collisions). Using Bayesian optimization, they struck a balance between accuracy and network size suitable for FPGAs — programmable chips that operate in real time. Top architectures, including fully connected, convolutional, and hybrid ones, achieved a transverse energy resolution of about 80 MeV, outperforming the traditional optimal filtering method. They additionally implemented Deep Evidential Regression, which estimates uncertainty for each event without increasing network complexity.
threads: В коллайдере частиц какофония сигналов заглушает важные события, как крики толпы скрывают шёпот. Инженеры создали нейросеть-«суперслух», которая работает прямо в детекторе, точно измеряет энергию и честно признаётся, когда сомневается. Это приближает разгадку тёмной материи.

title: Quantum Simulator Discovers Plasma Memory
oneliner: In a quantum simulator, particles flew apart like an explosion but retained memory of the initial charge clumps.
description: Physicists simulated a collision of charged particles. Contrary to expectations, instead of chaos, a plasma emerged, expanding as a shock wave and retaining memory of the initial charge clumps. The reason — plasma oscillations akin to a pendulum. This discovery helps understand matter under extreme
abstract: Using a high-precision quantum simulator, physicists investigated the dynamics of gauge fields at high energies. Contrary to expected thermalization, a system of particle-antiparticle pairs generated ballistic plasma and retained memory of charge clusters — like a wave spreading from a stone rather than vanishing instantly. The cause was undamped plasma oscillations between the electric field and current in the Schwinger model (simplified quantum electrodynamics), which exist even in the continuum limit; using Wigner distributions, scientists visualized them and obtained experimentally confirmed predictions.
threads: В квантовом симуляторе сгусток заряженных частиц не расплылся хаотично, а разлетелся прямыми потоками, помня о начальной форме. Такой баллистический плазменный сюрприз меняет понимание высокоэнергетических процессов и может пролить свет на тайны ранней Вселенной.

title: Tiny black holes from cosmic collisions
oneliner: Collisions of ultra-high-energy particles in space can give birth to tiny black holes, paving the way to understanding gravity at the atomic scale.
description: When particle rays collide in space, they can create microscopic black holes that instantly evaporate into a stream of neutrinos. Already, by studying neutrinos from a distant galaxy, scientists have tested energy limits where gravity changes the rules. Upcoming observatories will search even deeper
abstract: Cosmic rays pack energies unreachable on Earth, turning them into a natural lab for quantum gravity. The authors explored three paths: black holes born in active galactic cores, their evaporation, and gravitons leaking in from extra dimensions. Neutrino data from blazar TXS 0506+056 set a lower bound: fundamental gravity scale M_f > 0.3 TeV (for perspective, the LHC runs at 13 TeV). Smash cosmic rays together near supermassive black holes, and that limit could leap to a mind-boggling 2 PeV. The unique neutrino fingerprint from evaporating black holes will be a smoking gun.
threads: Две космические частицы, столкнувшись, могут родить чёрную дыру размером с атомное ядро. Она тут же испарится, вспыхнув нейтрино. Астрофизики уже ищут такие вспышки в данных нейтринных телескопов: если найдут — это укажет на скрытые измерения пространства.

title: Glowing Atoms Create a Nearly Perfect Engine
oneliner: When atoms shine in sync, a heat engine with near-perfect efficiency is born.
description: Synchronized radiation from atoms has led to a new type of heat engine: power grows explosively, and efficiency is near 100%. This paves the way for ultra-miniature devices with fantastic energy savings.
abstract: Physicists have proposed a quantum heat engine based on a set of N atoms. Like rowers synchronizing their strokes, the atoms harness collective effects of superradiance (enhanced emission) and superabsorption (enhanced absorption). The work cycle involves coordinated pumping and relaxation of the atoms from a single cold reservoir. Carefully designed pulses maintain adiabaticity, and the average power grows quadratically with system size: P ∝ N². The efficiency can approach unity, paving the way for scalable quantum heat machines.
threads: Группа атомов, вспыхивая синхронно, как стая светлячков, может создать двигатель с почти идеальной эффективностью. Ученые показали, что мощность такого квантового мотора растет квадратично с числом атомов, открывая путь к миниатюрным сверхэффективным устройствам.

title: Icy Wanderer Chiron Encircled by Rings and a Disk
oneliner: The tiny icy body Chiron sports a ring system—like its own miniature planetary system.
description: During an occultation of a star by Chiron, telescopes caught a series of blinks—three rings and a disk gave themselves away. The outermost ring lies in a zone where moons should be born, not rings. Comparison with old data shows the structure is changing, and we are witnessing the construction of a
abstract: The centaur Chiron, an object orbiting between Saturn and Uranus, delivered a surprise: during an occultation of a distant star (when the body blocks its light), three narrow rings were discovered at distances of 273, 325, and 438 km from its center. The outer ring lies beyond the Roche limit—the boundary where tidal forces tear bodies apart; perhaps this material is forming moons. Chiron became the fourth small body in the Solar System known to have rings. Comparison with past observations revealed that the structure is changing, and scientists are witnessing the evolution of a ring system in real time for the first time.
threads: Небесное тело Хирон, всего 200 км в поперечнике, обзавёлся кольцами. Астрономы, наблюдая за его прохождением перед звездой, заметили сложный узор из трёх колец, пылевого диска и далёкого объекта — возможно, так рождается новый спутник.

title: Supernovae Point to a Changing Dark Energy
oneliner: Accounting for the age of supernova progenitor stars reveals that dark energy is likely not constant, but changes over time.
description: It was previously thought that Type Ia supernovae were reliable 'standard candles' for measuring distances, their brightness independent of age. New research shows: old stars produce dimmer explosions. This distorts the cosmic ruler. Correcting for age removes the discrepancy, and the data leans tow
abstract: Type Ia supernovae are cosmology’s ‘standard candles.’ But a study found their calibrated brightness drops with the age of the parent star (5.5σ correlation). The galaxy mass correction doesn’t fix this, introducing a redshift-dependent systematic shift. After accounting for the age trend, supernova data better fit the w0waCDM model with evolving dark energy, earlier proposed by DESI. Combining with baryon acoustic oscillations and the cosmic microwave background pushes the tension with ΛCDM to >9σ, hinting the universe may not be accelerating now.
threads: Старые звёзды взрываются тусклее — это как если бы старые свечи давали меньше света. Астрономы обнаружили, что возраст прародителей сверхновых искажает измерение космических расстояний. После поправки оказалось: тёмная энергия, возможно, меняется, и ускорение Вселенной могло остановиться.

title: Two Probes Will Dive into the Tail of an Interstellar Comet
oneliner: In late October 2025, the Europa Clipper and Hera spacecraft will find themselves inside the tail of Comet 3I/ATLAS.
description: A comet from another star system will pass by two Earth spacecraft. In late October, they will spend several days in its tail—this is the first chance to directly study extrasolar material.
abstract: In October-November 2025, the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS — a visitor from another star system — will pass close to the trajectories of the Europa Clipper and Hera probes. Thanks to a rare alignment, their paths may intersect the comet’s ion tail — a plasma stream stretched out by the solar wind. This would let the instruments directly register the tail’s composition, changes in the solar wind, and the characteristic magnetic field that envelops the comet like a cloak. Such an experiment is like taking a sample of material from another star’s nursery without leaving the Solar System.
threads: В конце октября две межпланетные станции 'нырнут' в хвост кометы, прилетевшей от другой звезды. Это первая возможность в истории 'пощупать' инозвёздное вещество прямо в космосе. Приборы зафиксируют, как невидимый шлейф частиц окутывает зонды, рассказывая о химии далёких миров.

title: Quantum Mind? Can't Do Without the Classical
oneliner: Quantum systems cannot think and act purposefully on their own.
description: Scientists have proved: purely quantum systems cannot become agents. To make plans and decisions, you need to copy information about the world, and the quantum no-cloning theorem puts an end to such attempts. The mind requires classical 'parts'. This explains how familiar behavior emerges in a quant
abstract: What does it take to have agency — the ability to act purposefully? Three conditions: a model of the world, evaluation of options, and selection of the best action. It turns out that quantum systems on their own cannot fulfill them. The no-cloning theorem prevents copying information for the model and comparing options, and linearity of evolution prevents selective action. Even approximate cloning doesn't help. Conclusion: agency requires classical resources — this explains the emergence of classical agents in a quantum universe and the limitations of quantum computers for modeling behavior.
threads: Квантовые частицы не могут копировать сами себя. Это не причуда, а математический запрет. Он не даёт чисто квантовой системе мыслить и принимать решения — для разума обязательно нужен кусочек классического мира. Возможно, наш мозг — смесь того и другого.

title: Noise That Helps: A Quantum 'Pendulum' on a Chip
oneliner: Errors on an IBM quantum chip helped create a stable time crystal — rhythmic oscillations that never fade.
description: Scientists simulated a magnetic material with a kagome pattern on a quantum computer. They found that noise from extra qubits doesn't disrupt but actually prolongs periodic oscillations (a time crystal), and sometimes even creates them. This is a way to control quantum states using unavoidable error
abstract: With IBM's superconducting processors, researchers simulated a quantum time crystal on a kagome lattice, weaving in complex topology via ancilla qubits. The structured noise from these qubits unexpectedly stabilized long-lived subharmonic oscillations—as if interference makes a pendulum swing more rhythmically. Adding charge pumping at the edge created a special, localized time crystal at the boundary. Without the pump, that same noise spontaneously generated an orderly rhythm, revealing a handy way to control out-of-equilibrium states on noisy quantum hardware.
threads: Шум, который обычно мешает квантовым системам, на чипе IBM сыграл роль дирижёра: он заставил «маятник» качаться дольше, создав подобие вечного тиканья. Этот трюк может сделать квантовые компьютеры стабильнее, превратив ошибки из врагов в союзников.

title: Quantum Bounce: How the Universe Became Uniform
oneliner: After a quantum bounce, the universe’s irregularities vanish on their own — explaining why the cosmos is so smooth.
description: Scientists have proposed a model where the universe didn’t explode from a point but first contracted and then bounced. Quantum effects during the bounce smooth out any distortions, making space uniform and the same in all directions. This is a natural explanation for the cosmos’s smoothness, requiri
abstract: In modified loop quantum cosmology (mLQC-I), the dynamics of a universe with different expansion rates in different directions (anisotropy) have been studied. A new natural smoothing mechanism has been discovered: after the quantum bounce (which replaces the Big Bang), anisotropy is quickly suppressed on its own, without fine-tuning, for any type of matter. It's like a sea wave smoothing out ripples in the sand. This result explains why our universe is so homogeneous and sets mLQC-I apart from other bounce models.
threads: До Большого взрыва Вселенная могла сжиматься, а затем — отскочить. Ученые выяснили, что при таком отскоке все неровности сами собой разглаживаются, как складки на простыне при рывке. Вселенная становится идеально ровной без всякой настройки — загадка однородности решена.

title: Social Laser: Why We Act in Sync
oneliner: Disconnected people act as one—just like light in a laser.
description: Scientists applied the laser principle to crowd behavior. People are like atoms: they get charged by news and slogans. Once enough charge builds up, a single impulse triggers synchronized action. The model promises to predict mass events.
abstract: Social laser theory expands quantum-like modeling—using quantum math to model thought and behavior—with ideas from quantum field theory. Think of society as a swarm of 'social atoms' that soak up and emit chunks of social energy. Once we hit a state like population inversion in a laser, external triggers—calls to action, breaking news—unleash a coherent surge: protests or ideological pivots. The theory points to measurable signals that could help diagnose and forecast social dynamics.
threads: В лазере атомы вспыхивают разом, превращая разрозненный свет в направленный луч. Оказывается, общество иногда ведёт себя так же: когда достаточно людей «заряжено» одной идеей, сигнал из медиа может заставить всех действовать синхронно. Это объясняет, почему протесты или тренды вспыхивают внезапно, и намекает на возможность их предсказывать.

title: Coin-sized camera spots distant planets
oneliner: A 15 mm lens on the Hayabusa2 probe first spotted distant planets passing across their stars.
description: Scientists pointed the Japanese probe's navigation camera at alien suns and saw giant planets briefly dimming their light. Even such tiny optics, with clever data processing, can rival space telescopes. This trick promises to find planets that have been hiding for years.
abstract: The navigation camera of Japan's Hayabusa2 probe, with a lens just 15 mm (smaller than many smartphones), has confidently recorded exoplanet transits for the first time. For each event, the mid-transit time was measured with an accuracy of up to 6 minutes, and the planetary parameters matched those from the specialized TESS telescope within 3%. This is a record for the smallest optics working in space, and it shows that even tiny instruments can search for rare long-period planets — much like a miniature sonar spotting a massive whale in the ocean.
threads: Камера размером с крупную монету на борту японского зонда «Хаябуса-2» впервые уверенно засекла проход далёких планет-гигантов по дискам своих звёзд. Этот успех означает, что крошечные инструменты смогут открывать неуловимые долгопериодические миры.

title: Stellar explosions puff up giant gamma-ray bubbles
oneliner: It’s not a black hole, but countless star explosions—like a giant cauldron boiling.
description: A computer model revealed that the gamma-ray bubbles at the Galaxy’s center are not the result of a black hole, but of vigorous stellar life. Like a boiling pot, massive star explosions hurl out particles that shine in gamma rays. The finding explained the Fermi telescope’s riddle and showed where t
abstract: For the first time, it has been numerically shown that bilobed gamma-ray structures like the Fermi bubbles can form solely from star formation and supernovae, without invoking an active galactic nucleus. Simulations with full magnetohydrodynamics and cosmic-ray transport predict a fluctuating spectrum: when leptonic processes dominate (inverse Compton), excellent agreement with Fermi telescope data is achieved. Soft X-ray and synchrotron radio signals are also predicted, linking cosmic-ray physics to the galactic outskirts.
threads: Считалось, что гигантские гамма-пузыри в нашей Галактике надувает сверхмассивная чёрная дыра. Однако компьютерная симуляция воспроизвела их без неё. Оказалось, хватает обычных взрывов звёзд. Это открытие сулит новые способы изучать бурную жизнь галактик.

title: Resistance Paints the Secret Angles of a Magnetic Dance
oneliner: In thin films, a strange shift reveals the hidden behavior of magnetic arrows.
description: In bismuth ferrite coated with platinum, the resistance twists unpredictably—each microscopic magnetic arrow inside seems to point in its own direction. This glitch, never observed before, hints at the complex life of magnetic domains. It could lead to new memory based on magnetization vortices.
abstract: Using the spin Hall effect method (SMR), the influence of the direction of an external magnetic field on the resistance of a platinum-multiferroic (BiFeO3) system was studied. Typically, for ferromagnets the signal phase shift is 0°, for antiferromagnets — 90°. In this work, it was discovered that in epitaxial BiFeO3 films this shift can take on a wide variety of values, varying from sample to sample. This deviation from the pattern is like a magnetic 'fingerprint' unique to each sample, even though the composition is the same. This points to complex magnetic interactions at the interface and calls for new explanations.
threads: Вы включаете ток в тонкой магнитной плёнке, а сопротивление рисует карту спинов. Обычно карта простая, но в новом материале она сбилась – словно компас показывает не на север. Такие аномалии сулят прорыв в спинтронике.

title: Tantalum Nucleus Reveals Electric Asymmetry
oneliner: Tantalum’s nucleus: a tiny skew that saved the Universe.
description: An electric dipole moment has been spotted in the tantalum nucleus — like a spinning top’s center of mass suddenly shifting. This imbalance, measured with record precision, sheds light on the mystery of missing antimatter and aids the hunt for dark matter.
abstract: A nonzero electric dipole moment (EDM) of an atomic nucleus has been measured for the first time—for the isotope tantalum-181. The experiment used a method that tracks the change in superconducting current (proposed in 2016). Best value |d_e| = (3.39 ± 0.31)·10⁻³² e·cm (statistical uncertainty), and considering systematic uncertainties it remains positive with 99.985% confidence. For the control nucleus lead-207, only an upper limit of about 1.2·10⁻³¹ e·cm was set. Measuring such a tiny quantity—like detecting a shift a thousandth of a proton’s diameter—opens the way to test theories beyond the Standard Model and the causes of the baryon asymmetry of the Universe.
threads: Крошечная асимметрия в атомном ядре тантала указала на нарушение симметрии времени. Возможно, именно такие эффекты когда-то позволили материи победить антиматерию и создать нашу Вселенную. Новый метод измерения настолько чувствителен, что улавливает сдвиг заряда, сравнимый с шевелением пылинки на другом конце галактики.

title: The Grand Piano Chip: How Light and Sound Learned to Work Together Without Noise
oneliner: A rigidly fixed design dissipates heat 60 times better, allowing light and sound to interact cleanly.
description: Engineers unveiled a chip that’s not suspended but firmly anchored, dramatically improving heat dissipation. Heating distortions are reduced by a factor of 60, and quantum sound vibrations persist even under intense laser light. The technology opens the door to efficient microwave-to-light converter
abstract: Typical suspended optomechanical chips suffer from overheating and noise. A release-free silicon structure has been developed for cryogenic temperatures. It suppresses light-induced heating by 18 dB and preserves the quantum state of mechanical oscillations at 3000 times higher optical energy. Measurements revealed that the initial heating is limited by the mechanical damping time. It’s like swapping a swaying suspension bridge for a massive slab: heat is dissipated more efficiently, and there’s less noise. This approach is promising for quantum frequency converters, such as from microwaves to optics.
threads: Свет и звук плохо уживаются на микрочипах: малейший нагрев от лазера вызывает шум. Инженеры создали чип, который отводит тепло в 60 раз лучше, снижая искажения. Теперь можно подавать мощный свет без помех для квантовых звуковых волн. Это шаг к идеальным преобразователям микроволны-свет — основе квантового интернета.

title: A Quantum Instrument Plays the Music of Primes
oneliner: Physicists have created a quantum instrument whose notes exactly mirror the numbers that hold the secret of prime numbers.
description: Scientists constructed a quantum energy landscape whose allowed levels match the special points of the Riemann zeta function, which governs the distribution of primes. Corrections to the approximate model obeyed a simple rule that explains a mysterious fractal pattern and brings us closer to proving
abstract: A quantum potential has been constructed whose energy levels perfectly match the imaginary parts of the first non-trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function—numbers inextricably linked to the mystery of prime numbers. The starting approximation was the smooth Riemann–von Mangoldt formula, to which correction functions were added. It turned out that the corrections follow a simple pattern determined by the error of this approximation—like ripples spreading from an inaccuracy. This result explains the fractal landscape of the potential previously discovered by Wu and Sprung, and hints at the arithmetic roots of quantum chaos.
threads: У чисел-призраков, скрывающих тайну простых чисел, нашёлся квантовый двойник. Учёные смоделировали систему, чья энергетическая гамма в точности повторяет нули дзета-функции. За регулярными поправками стоит простая математическая ошибка, и это может стать ключом к разгадке великой гипотезы.

title: Gravitational Waves Gain Overtones
oneliner: After merging, black holes ring in a chord: for the first time, a nonlinear overtone of gravitational waves is isolated.
description: Event GW250114 helped physicists capture not only the main wave from the black hole merger, but also the quiet overtones. These nonlinear oscillations, born from the self-interaction of spacetime, refined the black hole’s parameters and opened a new way to test relativity in strong fields.
abstract: In event GW250114, scientists analyzed the 'ringdown' of the newly formed black hole. Using Bayesian statistics, they compared a linear model (the fundamental tone) with a nonlinear one (which included the overtone 220Q, predicted by General Relativity). The nonlinear model got a higher Bayes factor and yielded mass and spin values much closer to numerical simulation results. This strongly shows that in strong gravity, nonlinear effects are important — much like how a bell’s overtones define its sound. This discovery opens a new way to test gravity theory in extreme conditions where linear approximations break down.
threads: При слиянии двух чёрных дыр пространство-время звенит не одной чистой нотой, а с тихими обертонами. В событии GW250114 физики впервые чётко расслышали нелинейные гармоники — это открыло способ тестировать теорию Эйнштейна в самых суровых уголках Вселенной.

title: Earth's Shadow Hunts for Alien Probes
oneliner: Scientists have proposed a new way to search for extraterrestrial artifacts, using Earth's shadow as a filter.
description: Astronomers tested the idea of using Earth's shadow to detect alien probes. An automated telescope captured brief light flashes near the shadow's edge — objects not listed in any catalog. This could change the approach to the search for extraterrestrial intelligence.
abstract: The search for extraterrestrial artifacts in the Solar System long lingered on the sidelines of science, but modern automated sky surveys are opening up new possibilities. The authors explore four approaches: from analyzing old photographic plates to studying the spectra of objects in orbit. The key method uses Earth's shadow as a natural filter: inside the shadow, the sky is darker, making faint artificial objects easier to spot. Tests on ZTF images revealed uncatalogued fast-transient phenomena near the shadow's center — potential traces of alien probes. Earth's shadow is becoming a new testing ground for the search for extraterrestrial intelligence.
threads: Астрономы впервые применили тень Земли как естественный фильтр для поиска инопланетных зондов. Обычно объекты вблизи Солнца теряются в его лучах, но тень позволяет их увидеть. В данных телескопа ZTF нашли несколько загадочных быстрых вспышек — возможно, это искусственные аппараты.

title: Quantum ions no longer need freezing cold
oneliner: Smooth laser tuning lets qubits operate accurately even with noticeable jitter, ditching complex cooling.
description: Ion-based quantum computers usually need near absolute zero to suppress particle jitter. A new “smooth gate” method sidesteps extreme cooling: changing the laser frequency during the operation itself cancels out excess jitters. It’s like gently stopping a swinging swing with a single precise push. E
abstract: The 'smooth gate' method for trapped-ion qubits eliminates spin-motional coupling errors by smoothly varying the field parameters. The fidelity of two-qubit operations reached 99.992% without deep cooling, and the error remained below 0.05% even at high ion temperatures (up to ~9 phonons). Like a jeweler who skillfully smooths out shakiness, this approach enables simpler and faster quantum processors operating at moderate temperatures.
threads: Крошечный ион в ловушке дрожит — и ошибается в квантовых расчётах. Но если плавно менять лазерный импульс, дрожь затихает сама, без ледяного охлаждения. Такой трюк открывает дверь к простым и надёжным квантовым компьютерам.

title: Magnetic Vortex Becomes a Quantum Bit
oneliner: In granular superconductors, magnetic vortices behave like two-state quantum systems.
description: In ordinary superconductors, magnetic vortices waste energy and cause interference. A film of aluminum grains traps a vortex in place, turning it into an ultrastable quantum bit. Scientists control it with microwaves, paving the way for new quantum computers and sensors.
abstract: Think of ordinary Abrikosov vortices in superconductors—they leak energy and act like spinning tops that eventually wind down. But in grainy superconducting films, vortices get trapped and suddenly play by quantum rules: they become two-level systems, like natural qubits, with coherence lasting microseconds and relaxation up to fractions of a millisecond. By using circuit QED tricks, researchers can now flip these vortex states and read them out without disturbing them. It’s like artificial atoms born from disorder, paving the way for quantum computers and supersensitive detectors.
threads: Магнитные вихри в гранулированном алюминии ведут себя как квантовые биты — почти без потерь и с долгой памятью. Из них можно строить устойчивые квантовые компьютеры и датчики с атомной точностью.

title: Only Quantum Gravity Can Entangle Masses
oneliner: Regular gravity can't entangle two distant objects—quantum spacetime is required.
description: The study settles the debate: Einstein's classical gravity cannot create quantum entanglement between massive bodies. Only if spacetime itself acquires quantum properties does such a connection become possible. This is an important step toward a theory uniting gravity and the quantum world.
abstract: Recently, a paper claimed that classical gravity could entangle two massive superpositions via local effects. The authors of this study refute that, proving: if gravity can create entanglement at a distance, then it must be quantum. This sheds light on the fundamental question of gravity's nature. Interesting fact: even if gravity acted instantaneously, that wouldn't be enough—you need the quantum 'jitter' of the field. The result is important for future experiments testing quantum gravity.
threads: Два массивных шара в космосе: толкните один, и второй не почувствует толчок мгновенно — гравитационные волны идут с запозданием. Но квантовая запутанность — это не волна, а нечто иное. Спор о том, способна ли обычная гравитация создать такую связь, разрешён: без квантового поля не обойтись.

title: How a Brainless Slime Mold Outpaces Supercomputers
oneliner: An ordinary amoeba, lacking a nervous system, can perform an astronomical number of computations — all thanks to the movement of its gelatinous mass.
description: Scientists have determined how many computational operations Physarum polycephalum — a slime mold devoid of neurons — can perform. By analyzing its growth and shape changes, they applied the physical limit of computation speed imposed by energy. It turns out that in a day, this blob of slime perform
abstract: For the first time, researchers have gauged the computational limit of an organism without a nervous system—the slime mold Physarum polycephalum. By analyzing changes in its shape (area, perimeter, fractal dimension) and applying the Margolus–Levitin theorem (which links computation speed to available energy), they showed it can perform up to 10³⁶ logical operations in a day, using hydromechanical, chemical, and even quantum-optical degrees of freedom. Its computational power grows linearly with energy in a non-equilibrium steady state. This approach opens the door to comparing the computational abilities of diverse life forms—from bacteria to neural networks.
threads: Одна бесформенная капля слизи способна за день обработать больше информации, чем все компьютеры мира. Учёные выяснили, что амёба без мозга использует энергию на полную катушку, приближаясь к физическому пределу вычислений. Это открытие заставляет иначе взглянуть на природу мышления — может, разум вовсе не требует нейронов.

title: Quantum Communication Speeds Up Nearly Five Times
oneliner: Scientists have for the first time used a ten-light-channel trick to speed up entanglement between charged atoms.
description: Two charged atoms held in vacuum traps 1.2 km apart now become entangled almost five times faster by sending ten light pulses simultaneously — as if the post office dispatched ten delivery vans instead of one. This first-of-its-kind experiment with ion traps opens the door to a quantum internet.
abstract: For the first time, scientists have turbocharged the creation of quantum entanglement between two trapped-ion nodes by sending light signals in parallel batches—like throwing multiple lottery tickets into the draw at once. This made the connection 4.59 times faster, with 95.9% fidelity, over a distance of 1.2 km. Think of it as playing the lottery: the more tickets you enter, the quicker you hit the jackpot. The technique scales up easily, paving the way for distributed quantum computing.
threads: Квантовый интернет только что получил ускоритель. Физики заставили два иона «чувствовать» друг друга почти в пять раз быстрее, пропуская свет через десять временных каналов сразу — как многополосное шоссе для фотонов. Это первый такой трюк с ионными ловушками, и он обещает сделать квантовые сети намного быстрее, приближая эру защищённой связи и сверхмощных распределённых вычислений.

title: Superfluid Helium Feels How the Earth Spins
oneliner: A lab experiment will capture how Earth twists space-time.
description: Earth drags the fabric of space-time along with it — an effect predicted by Einstein. Scientists propose catching it with superfluid helium and a highly precise detector. The device will notice rotation slower than one revolution in the entire history of Earth.
abstract: The frame-dragging effect — one of the predictions of general relativity — is still difficult to measure locally. A lab experiment has been proposed using a gyroscope based on superfluid helium-4 and a Josephson junction, leveraging macroscopic quantum properties. At a temperature of 10 millikelvin, the expected noise level will be 5×10⁻¹⁷ rad/s/√Hz, allowing the dragging rate to be measured with 0.2% accuracy in one second. This sensitivity corresponds to detecting one rotation in 4 billion years, or a proper time difference of 10⁻³⁵ seconds — at the threshold of Planck scales.
threads: Земля не просто крутится — она увлекает за собой ткань пространства, как ложка вязкий мед. Ученые разработали эксперимент со сверхтекучим гелием, чтобы уловить этот почти неуловимый эффект. Прибор настолько чувствителен, что может заметить поворот на один оборот за 4 миллиарда лет.

title: The Secret to Flawless Neon Ice for Quantum Computers
oneliner: Microwave resonators test neon films, laying the groundwork for error-free qubits.
description: Scientists grow smooth layers of frozen neon and check them with microwave sensors. A brief warm-up to 12 K makes the film uniform, allowing electrons to levitate over it as low-error qubits. This simple method paves the way for reliable quantum processors.
abstract: Electrons hovering over frozen neon are promising qubits, but they need a smooth neon surface to work well. The researchers used an array of superconducting microwave resonators as sensitive probes to track shifts in resonant frequency and internal quality factor (the ability to store energy) during film growth. Pulsed gas deposition near neon's triple point yielded reproducible layers, and a quick bake at 12 K significantly improved uniformity—much like annealing glass to relieve internal stresses. This resonator-based metrology paves the way for precise in-situ control, bringing us closer to quantum chips based on electrons above inert solids.
threads: Твёрдый неон похож на идеально гладкий каток для электронов. Но малейшая рябь плёнки порождает ошибки в кубитах. Физики нашли способ выравнивать неоновый лёд, прогревая его до -261 °C, и проверять гладкость микроволновым «эхом». Это шаг к квантовым чипам, которые работают на левитирующих электронах.

title: Encounter with a Shutter: How a Photon Creates a Fountain of Probabilities
oneliner: A single photon, colliding with a shutter, gives rise not to a half, but to a fountain of probabilities.
description: When a single photon interrupts a fast shutter, it cannot be cut, and a mixture of states with different photon numbers emerges. Locally, it appears as a photon on the left and darkness on the right with a thin transition zone. This effect deepens our understanding of light and could improve precisi
abstract: If you try to 'cut off' a piece of a photon with an optical shutter, you don’t get another photon, nor a mixture of a photon and vacuum. Instead, a complex quantum state emerges—a superposition (overlay) and mixture of states with any number of photons, from zero to infinity. Amazingly, locally, to the left and right of a narrow transition region, this state is equivalent to a single photon or vacuum, respectively. That is, on one side we see a photon, and on the other—emptiness. This work shows that even a simple attempt to block light can generate an infinite variety of quantum states from a single photon.
threads: Одиночный фотон летит на камеру, но затвор захлопывается прямо перед ним. Разрезать его нельзя, и рождается квантовая смесь — бесконечный набор состояний с разным числом частиц. Слева свет, справа тьма, а между ними — парадоксальный переход. Такая игра со светом обещает новые приборы.

title: Dark Energy Is Not Constant: A New Look at the Universe's Acceleration
oneliner: Data from the DESI survey suggests that the mysterious force speeding up the cosmos may not be eternal or constant.
description: Analysis of galaxy maps, quasar light, and supernovae shows that the simple model of constant dark energy is losing to a version with a smoothly changing force. This needs verification, but it's intriguing.
abstract: Fresh data from the DESI spectroscopic survey have allowed the study of dark energy through baryon acoustic oscillations—the 'echo' of the early Universe, frozen in the distribution of galaxies and clouds of intergalactic gas. Scientists compared dark energy models with a constant and a changing equation of state. The dynamical variant received the highest preference (up to 3.1σ for a combination of data), but the result strongly depends on the set of observations—adding supernovae weakens the signal. It resembles a detective story where evidence points to a suspect, but not all the clues agree.
threads: Космос расширяется, и что-то неведомое толкает его всё сильнее. Новые замеры с невиданной точностью показали: тёмная энергия не стоит на месте, она меняется. Если это подтвердится, наши учебники по астрономии придётся переписывать.

title: The Light Chip: A Treasure Map in a Sea of Chaos
oneliner: A new method turns the cryptic blueprints of light microchips into a clear map, highlighting the zones that make or break the device.
description: Engineers are leaning on computers more to design minuscule light circuits, but the output can be a whimsical mosaic. Now, scientists have a tool that shines a light on the weakest links, preventing expensive production flops.
abstract: Inverse design (automatic optimization) creates ultra-compact photonic circuits, but it generates non-obvious shapes that are sensitive to defects. To solve this problem, researchers trained a surrogate neural network to predict device performance and applied the integrated gradients (IG) method to build pixel-level importance maps. It turned out that 'hot' spots exactly correspond to physically meaningful elements — splitters and sharp bends. Experiments confirmed: point defects in these zones increase losses up to 11 times more than in neutral areas. It's like bridge diagnostics: knowing the most stressed beams, you can reinforce them in advance.
threads: Световые чипы, спроектированные компьютером, выглядят как хаотичная мозаика. Новый метод превращает их в карту сокровищ: подсвечивает пиксели, без которых всё перестаёт работать. Испытания доказали: повреждение «золотых» точек увеличивает потери сигнала в 11 раз. Так рождается язык, на котором нейросети объясняют свои загадочные решения.

title: Quantum Engines Powered by a Look
oneliner: Detecting a particle passing through a barrier can provide energy and even cool the system.
description: A quantum particle can seep through an energy barrier even if it lacks the strength to jump over it. If you place a detector right on the barrier, the very act of detecting the particle nudges it, taking away or adding energy. Thus, a microscopic engine emerges, running solely on observation—without
abstract: Quantum tunneling allows particles to overcome energy barriers. Measuring the position of an electron inside the barrier transfers it to a higher-energy state, opening the door to quantum engines. Such devices can simultaneously produce energy and cool, operating in a hybrid mode. Additionally, autonomous cooling driven solely by heat flow, without external voltage, has been demonstrated, along with the "noise-cleaning" effect, where measurement drives the system into a stable dark state. It's akin to how observation not only gathers information but actively reshapes the energy landscape.
threads: Частица проходит сквозь стену, а когда её замечают — получает пинок энергии, становясь крошечной электростанцией. Такие «двигатели наблюдения» смогут питать наноустройства или холодильники для квантовых компьютеров без единой батарейки — достаточно просто смотреть.

title: Black Holes Where You'd Least Expect Them: Solving the Mass Gap Mystery
oneliner: Gravitational waves revealed black holes with masses of 50–70 Suns — right in the 'empty' zone of the mass gap.
description: It was thought that the most massive stars explode completely, leaving no black holes, so none exist in the 50 to 130 solar mass range. New observations found several such anomalies with low spin. The reason lies in the nuclear reaction rate inside stars, which shifts the boundaries of what's possib
abstract: Astronomers have found in gravitational-wave data a new group of massive (50–70 solar masses) black holes with low spin, which are hard to explain by mergers of smaller objects. This pushes the previously assumed 'gap' boundary — the region where black holes shouldn't form due to pair-instability supernovae — to 68.5 solar masses. Surprisingly, the new value matches theoretical models if the rate of a key nuclear reaction (carbon capturing an alpha particle) is slightly higher than previously thought. So, the makeup of stellar interiors shapes the kind of black holes we get to see.
threads: Взрывы сверхновых па́рной нестабильности должны оставлять «пробел» в массах черных дыр. Но новые данные гравитационных волн показали дыры прямо в этом пробеле — словно рыбы в пересохшей реке. Это меняет наши представления о жизни и смерти самых тяжелых звезд.

title: How to Braid Atoms to Save a Quantum Battery
oneliner: Giant atoms braided together suppress energy leaks, making quantum batteries long-lasting.
description: Quantum batteries quickly lose energy due to interactions with the environment. Researchers suggest braiding giant atoms together — the leak channels then cancel each other out, allowing useful energy transfer without losses. This is a step toward durable quantum storage devices.
abstract: Quantum batteries lose energy due to decoherence (interaction with the environment), which causes their aging. The solution is giant atoms with non-local connections, braided into a 'braid', where noise channels, like opposing waves, cancel each other out, without interfering with the energy transfer from charger to battery. This braided configuration is more effective than separate or nested schemes. A method of directed charging, reversible by magnetic flux, is also proposed. The result is a recipe for long-lived quantum batteries.
threads: В квантовых батареях энергию крадёт шум, как невидимые трещинки в стакане. Физики придумали «косу» из гигантских атомов, где шум сам себя глушит. Такие батареи могут заряжаться без потерь и работать годами.

title: Whispering Chips: Quantum Sound for Hyper-Sensitive Gyroscopes
oneliner: Multiple taps on a chip force sound waves into a one-way whisper, slashing noise for ultra-sensitive rotation sensing.
description: Gyroscopes measure rotation but suffer from noise. A new chip design forces sound waves into a one-way flow, like whispers that travel only forward. By tapping the chip at multiple spots, backward-moving noise cancels out. The result is a gyroscope sensitive enough to detect the tiniest turns, usefu
abstract: Surface Acoustic Waves (SAWs) are elastic vibrations, like tiny earthquakes on a material's surface, and have been used in gyroscopes for decades to measure rotation. But the classic principle, based on the Coriolis force, no longer meets modern demands. Now, by using quantum SAWs (at the single-phonon level), scientists have shown that coupling through several spaced points creates a directional interaction that breaks reciprocity — like one-way traffic on a ring road. This dramatically boosts sensitivity and pulls the signal out of the noise.
threads: A gyroscope that hears rotation: on a tiny chip, sound waves are forced into a one-way lane, like a whisper traveling in only one direction around a dome. By tapping the wave at several points, noise nearly vanishes, revealing spins so faint they were once hidden. Soon, this quantum trick could steer drones and spacecraft with uncanny precision.

title: Tiny Seesaw Cooled Almost to Absolute Zero
oneliner: Physicists cooled a microscopic seesaw to 0.006 degrees above absolute zero—so cold that its swinging almost stopped, but not quite.
description: Using nuclear demagnetization—a method that extracts energy by removing a magnetic field—a 1.5-nanogram plate was passively cooled to 6.1 millikelvin. The residual jittering neatly obeyed the laws of thermodynamics. The record paves the way for gravitational wave detectors and testing quantum mechan
abstract: Mechanical resonators—tiny vibrating elements—are used for ultrasensitive measurements. To extend their coherence, physicists passively cooled a cantilever weighing 1.5 ng with a frequency of 700 Hz down to 6.1 mK using nuclear demagnetization. Even at such an extremely low temperature, thermal motion was clearly visible above the noise, and analysis confirmed its thermal nature. This result paves the way for cooling low-frequency resonators to sub-millikelvin temperatures, enabling new tests of quantum mechanics and improving detectors of very small forces.
threads: Крошечную балку весом в полтора нанограмма удалось остудить до 0,006 градуса выше абсолютного нуля. При такой температуре её колебания стали едва заметны, но всё ещё подчиняются тепловым законам. Это открывает путь к датчикам, способным уловить гравитационную волну или толчок от одной тёмной материи.

title: The Hunt for Moons of Rogue Planets
oneliner: For the first time, the James Webb Space Telescope tested a method to find moons around lonely planets drifting without stars.
description: Astronomers analyzed data on the orphan planet WISE 0855 — a dim, cold ball not far away. For 11 hours, Webb watched its light, trying to spot the shadow of a moon. No direct discovery happened, but the method proved its worth. If many rogue planets have Titan-sized moons, we will soon find them.
abstract: Astronomers with JWST searched for exomoons around the free-floating planet WISE 0855—a chilly world just 2.3 parsecs away. Over 11 hours, NIRSpec saw no transits, but a Gaussian process helped separate a moon signal from the planet’s own variability. Artificial tests showed 96% reliability for Titan-sized satellites (0.5% transit depth). With 18 such observations, the detection chance reaches 91% if moons are as common as Galilean ones. This is the first demonstration that JWST can find moons around starless planets—like an ultrasensitive detector catching fleeting shadows.
threads: У планет, летящих в темноте без звезды, могут быть свои луны. «Джеймс Уэбб» 11 часов всматривался в холодный шар WISE 0855 — не мелькнёт ли тень. Пока тихо, но доказано: если спутники есть, мы их скоро поймаем.

title: Electricity Reads Out Diamond Spins
oneliner: Scientists have found a way to read the quantum state of microscopic defects in diamond using electrical pulses — without complex optical setups.
description: The new method, CCDMR, replaces light with electrical signals to read quantum memory in diamond. It uses a process similar to developing photographic film: a laser writes spin information into electron traps, and then voltage and light release the charge, creating a measurable current. This opens th
abstract: For spin qubits in diamond, electrical readout is attractive due to its chip-level scalability, yet it has lagged behind optical methods in accuracy. The CCDMR protocol is proposed: the spin information of an NV center is converted into charge carriers, which are then trapped for a long time at the metal–diamond interface (Schottky barrier). Subsequent illumination and voltage release the charge, producing a photocurrent proportional to the initial spin. The method has been successfully tested after coherent control of single NV centers, combining the benefits of electrical detection with the stability of long-lived traps in wide-bandgap materials.
threads: Алмаз может стать сердцем квантового компьютера. Ученые нашли способ читать его квантовую память не светом, а электричеством — как батарейку. Скрытые заряды часами хранят информацию о спине, чтобы выдать её по первому требованию. Это шаг к квантовым чипам размером с ноготь.

title: Quantum Chip Outperforms Supercomputers: Two Hours vs. Years
oneliner: An experiment on a quantum processor showed it solves a specialized task in hours, while classical computers would take years.
description: Scientists created quantum circuits that produce a specific result with high probability. The H2 quantum processor tackled the most complex one in two hours. The best classical simulation methods on supercomputers would take several years. This is a practical leap in quantum supremacy and opens new
abstract: Peaked circuits have been created and tested on the Quantinuum H2 quantum processor—they directly output the desired bit string. An experiment with 2000 two-qubit gates took less than two hours; the Frontier classical supercomputer would need years (estimated using the best simulation methods). This demonstrates quantum advantage and serves as a benchmark for quantum devices. QCMA-completeness of the peakedness verification problem has also been proven, inspiring the creation of quantum-resistant encryption.
threads: Квантовый чип за два часа выдал результат, на повторение которого самым мощным суперкомпьютерам нужны годы. Это не магия, а квантовая интерференция: правильные ответы усиливаются, а неверные — гасят друг друга. Подобные схемы могут лечь в основу взломоустойчивого шифрования будущего.

title: A Black Hole Cooks Up Radiation Like a Pot of Soup
oneliner: A black hole’s hot radiation turns out to be a double copy of particles born cold in an electric field.
description: The double copy trick turns the cold birth of particles in a strong electric field into warm Hawking radiation. The horizon and temperature pop up by themselves, even though the original recipe doesn’t include them. This kitchen wizardry bridges gravity and the forces of the microworld.
abstract: Using the double copy method (which links gravity and gauge theory via the 'squares' of amplitudes), physicists have shown that the thermal spectrum of Hawking radiation during black hole collapse can be derived from the non-thermal production of particles in a background gauge field where there is no global horizon. This unifies quantum and classical approaches to describing black hole spacetime. Surprisingly, radiation that depends on a horizon emerges from a theory in which no horizon exists — like a shadow cast from another world.
threads: Физики нашли кулинарный секрет чёрных дыр. Метод «двойной копии» превращает простой рецепт супа (электрическое поле) в изысканное жаркое (гравитация). Оказалось, знаменитое излучение Хокинга — это всего лишь двойная копия рождения частиц в электричестве, где нет ни температуры, ни горизонта. Горячий эффект возникает из холодных ингредиентов. Такой трюк поможет склеить квантовый мир с тяготением.

title: Photo Editor for Quantum Computers
oneliner: The GANDALF algorithm cleans up noisy snapshots of atomic qubits, speeding up readout and reducing errors by a factor of 35.
description: Quantum computers based on neutral atoms are tripped up by slow state readout. The GANDALF program acts like a filter in a photo editor: it takes a short, noisy exposure and reconstructs a clear signal, doubles the speed of error correction, and slashes failures by tens of times.
abstract: Neutral atoms are a promising platform for quantum computing, but their weak spot is the slow readout of qubits (quantum bits). It takes milliseconds—way longer than the operations themselves—and bottlenecks error correction. The authors developed the GANDALF method, which uses a neural net to clean detector snapshots, like Photoshop removing noise from photos. This cut readout time by 1.6×, and combined with pipelined processing, reduced logical errors by 35× and shaved the full quantum error correction cycle time by 1.77× compared to the best alternatives. It’s like giving a quantum computer eagle eyes—fast and razor-sharp.
threads: Каждый кубит светится всего несколькими сотнями фотонов. Алгоритм, вдохновлённый фоторедакторами, угадывает состояние по зашумлённым снимкам. Квантовые компьютеры перестают ждать и начинают считать — с ошибками в 35 раз реже.

title: Star explosions accelerate particles to record energies
oneliner: New observations prove that supernova remnants can accelerate protons to energies hundreds of times higher than those of Earth's accelerators.
description: The LHAASO observatory studied gamma-ray emission from supernova remnant IC 443 and found that particles there are accelerated to record energies with no signs of slowing down. This is direct confirmation: it's star explosions that produce the most energetic cosmic rays bombarding Earth.
abstract: Supernova remnants (gas clouds after a star explosion) were long thought to be the main accelerators of cosmic rays, but direct evidence of acceleration to near-PeV energies was lacking. The LHAASO telescope studied gamma-ray emission from the remnant IC 443 and identified two sources. The spectrum of one extends beyond 30 TeV with no sign of a cutoff, indicating protons with energies around 300 TeV. It's like a sound wave bouncing back and forth, gradually pushing a particle to incredible speeds. This confirms that shock waves from stellar explosions are nature's super-accelerator.
threads: В китайском созвездии Тельца есть туманность — след от взрыва звезды. Оказывается, этот «звёздный пепел» работает как гигантский ускоритель, разгоняя протоны почти до скорости света. Энергия одной такой частицы в миллионы раз выше, чем в Большом адронном коллайдере. Теперь астрономы видят, что именно так рождаются космические лучи, непрерывно бомбардирующие Землю.

title: Quantum Predator-Prey Games
oneliner: Physicists had atoms act out the predator-prey drama on a quantum lattice.
description: The Lotka-Volterra model, familiar from the population fluctuations of foxes and hares, now works in the quantum world. Scientists lined up giant atoms and made them simulate population battles. Quantum effects don't disrupt but sustain these cycles — a path to the ultrafast simulators of the future
abstract: In a new study, a quantum analog of the Lotka–Volterra predator–prey model is proposed. On a two-dimensional lattice of Rydberg atoms (atoms with highly excited electrons), the authors showed that Rydberg excitations behave like populations of predators and prey, generating stable oscillations within microseconds. Quantum coherence triggers spontaneous symmetry breaking, while long-range interactions protect the cycles from desynchronization by quantum noise. Quantum jumps create "quasicycles" with amplitude decaying inversely proportional to the square root of the system size. This paves the way for quantum simulation of complex non-equilibrium phenomena.
threads: Зайцы и лисы — классика. Но что если в роли хищников и жертв выступят возбуждённые атомы? На квантовой решётке такие «гонки» идут за микросекунды и не сбиваются помехами. Это не просто игра — так инженеры учатся управлять неравновесными квантовыми системами для компьютеров будущего.

title: Quantum Leap: A Day Instead of 22 Years
oneliner: Calculations for turning carbon dioxide into fuel accelerated from 22 years to a single day.
description: Researchers sped up a quantum computer by a factor of 8000, making the conversion of CO₂ into fuel a reality. For the first time, such a machine outperformed a conventional one on a practical task.
abstract: For decades, simulating the conversion of carbon dioxide into fuel on a quantum computer seemed out of reach. The authors applied improvements across all components of the so-called fault-tolerant quantum stack — from error correction to algorithm compilation — and slashed the time from 22 years to just one day (a 7,900× speedup). This not only makes the calculation feasible but also predicts a quantum advantage over classical computers. The effect is like a well-rehearsed orchestra: when every musician perfects their part and plays in unison, the overall performance accelerates fantastically.
threads: 22 года — именно столько понадобилось бы квантовому компьютеру, чтобы найти способ превратить углекислый газ в топливо. Но инженеры нашли «секретный соус», ускорив расчёты до одного дня. Теперь зелёная энергия из воздуха — не фантастика, а реальный план на ближайшее будущее.

title: Diamond spies peek at magnetic secrets under Earth's core pressure
oneliner: Spy defects in diamond have captured superconductivity in titanium at 180 gigapascals for the first time.
description: Scientists have learned to create spy defects in diamond: swapping a carbon atom for nitrogen yields a sensor that responds to magnetic fields. For the first time, they've observed titanium under nearly two million atmospheres expel magnetic fields—a sure sign of superconductivity. This technology p
abstract: Nitrogen-vacancy centers in diamond are quantum sensors of magnetic fields at ultra-high pressures. A new method of creating them (ion implantation and pressure annealing) improved sensor density and stability, enabling measurements beyond 240 GPa. In a test experiment, the Meissner effect was observed in titanium at 180 GPa—a record observation of superconductivity at such compression. This paves the way for studying quantum phenomena in conditions close to Earth's core.
threads: Крошечные дефекты в алмазе, словно супермикрофоны, улавливают магнитные сигналы при давлении в миллионы атмосфер. Впервые удалось «услышать» сверхпроводимость в титане при 180 ГПа — это прорыв к созданию материалов, передающих энергию без потерь при обычных условиях.

title: Earth as a Lens for Searching Dark Matter
oneliner: A new method turns Earth into a gravitational telescope, focusing dark matter particles for detection.
description: By turning Earth into a gravitational lens, scientists found a way to focus dark matter particles into a single point. There, a laser makes them instantly convert into light, which is then caught by detectors. The method promises to detect even ultralight particles from thousands of light-years away
abstract: Earth's gravitational field can act as a lens, focusing dark matter particles (axion-like particles, ALPs) into a cosmic focal point. By aiming a laser beam there, we can stimulate their decay and detect the return signal. Simulations assuming ALP speeds of 520 km/s (from the S1 stellar stream) place the focus at about 9.6 billion meters (~25 lunar distances), with a sensitivity of g/M ~ 10⁻²² GeV⁻¹ for a source at 8 kpc. This lays the groundwork for future space telescopes capable of detecting dark matter beyond the Milky Way.
threads: Земля становится линзой: её гравитация фокусирует тёмную материю. Лазер подсвечивает невидимые частицы, заставляя их сверкнуть. Учёные рассчитали, что так можно поймать сигнал даже из далёких уголков галактики.

title: Chaos Without Disorder: Why Entropy Doesn't Grow
oneliner: Scientists have discovered a quantum system where chaos doesn't inflate disorder.
description: In the quantum world, chaos usually means an increase in entropy — a measure of disorder. But physicists have found an exception: a many-particle system remains chaotic, yet its entropy barely exceeds a tiny value. The reason is a rule akin to restricting the movement of cooks in a cramped kitchen.
abstract: It's a common belief that quantum chaos inflates entanglement. But this paper gives a counterexample: a system with Rydberg blockades, whose Wigner-Dyson level statistics and thermalization scream 'total chaos', yet it follows an area law for entanglement with a cap at ln2. The trick is in the Hilbert space: the blockades clamp the Schmidt rank at two. The authors generalize via a duality with quantum walks on median graphs and serve up a recipe to build systems with any desired entanglement ceiling. The bottom line: entanglement entropy is a lousy chaos detector; the geometry of quantum states is what counts.
threads: На шумной кухне хаос обычно растёт как снежный ком. Но добавьте одно правило: два повара не могут заходить в один проход. И внезапно беспорядок застывает на нуле, сколько бы народу ни было. Учёные нашли такой же эффект в квантовом мире: система из множества частиц буквально заперта в состоянии с крошечной энтропией, хотя ведёт себя абсолютно хаотично. Это открытие может привести к созданию сверхстабильных квантовых устройств.

title: Chimeric Materials: Magnetic Expulsion Without Superconductivity
oneliner: Physicists have found a way to create materials that expel a magnetic field even if they are not superconductors—this overturns our ideas about phases of matter.
description: The discovery shows that the expulsion of a magnetic field, the hallmark of superconductors, can occur in ordinary conductors and even insulators. In such 'chimeric' materials, opposite properties coexist. Experiments on networks of superconducting switches promise practical magnetic shields.
abstract: Researchers introduced the concept of chimeric states of matter, where broken and unbroken symmetries coexist. Using effective field theory, they showed that the Meissner effect (expulsion of a magnetic field), considered a hallmark of superconductivity, can arise in resistive materials and even insulators when an electric field is applied. This means that matter can exhibit superconducting and resistive properties simultaneously, depending on how it is measured. For realizing such states, networks of Josephson junctions are proposed. Like a chimera, the material blends traits of different phases within a single body.
threads: Обычный металл выталкивает магнитное поле, как сверхпроводник — без всякого охлаждения до космического холода. Это не фокус, а новое химерное состояние вещества, где порядок и беспорядок уживаются вместе. Такие материалы позволят управлять магнитными полями при комнатной температуре, открывая путь к простым магнитным экранам и левитации.

title: How to Find Life: Look for Molecular Diversity
oneliner: A new method for detecting life assesses not specific substances, but the chemical diversity of a sample.
description: To find life beyond Earth, you don't need to guess what it's made of. A new method evaluates not specific molecules, but the overall chemical diversity of a sample. Living organisms typically produce a rich cocktail of amino acids (the building blocks of proteins) and fatty acids, while non-living n
abstract: A new type of biosignature is proposed, based not on detecting individual molecules, but on the statistical organization of molecular ensembles. Analysis of amino acids and fatty acids has shown that biotic (life-related) samples are consistently more diverse than abiotic ones, and this pattern persists even after simulating cosmic degradation. The method, reminiscent of recognizing life's "chemical handwriting", uses only relative concentrations and is applicable to data from any planetary missions—from archival to future ones.
threads: Если в вашем супе плавает тысяча разных ингредиентов, его наверняка готовил живой повар, а не автомат. Похоже, что жизнь всегда создаёт химическое разнообразие, а безжизненная природа — однообразие. Учёные предлагают искать инопланетян именно так: не конкретные молекулы, а богатую смесь. Это может сработать даже для жизни, которая совсем не похожа на нашу.

title: A Quantum Turnstile Chip for Photons
oneliner: Physicists have created a microchip turnstile for light particles: it strictly allows passage in one direction, without destroying their quantum nature.
description: Ordinary light spreads in both directions, like a stream through an open door. The new chip acts as a turnstile—photons move only forward, and their quantum properties aren't erased. The technology will become the foundation for interference-resistant quantum networks.
abstract: For the first time, optical nonreciprocity—the property of passing light only in one direction—has been demonstrated for single photons without using magnets. This was achieved through noiseless light frequency conversion on a compact photonic chip. The device acts as an isolator and circulator, preserving quantum entanglement and coherence of photons, with record-breaking performance: losses of just 0.8 dB, back-signal suppression of 34 dB, and 97% fidelity. Just as a diode steers current in electronics, this 'photonic diode' paves the way for secure quantum networks and directional quantum communication.
threads: Учёные создали микрочип, работающий как турникет для частиц света: пропускает их только в одну сторону и сохраняет квантовую чистоту. Это шаг к защищённому от помех квантовому интернету.

title: Particles with Memory: A Step Toward an Invulnerable Quantum Computer
oneliner: Physicists have swapped mysterious half-particles for the first time—a key ingredient for future ultra-reliable quantum computers.
description: Ordinary particles don't change when swapped. But there are special 'knotty' ones that remember the path. For twenty years, scientists hunted for a variety called Majorana zero modes, crucial for quantum computing. In a new experiment on a microdevice, they performed a swap for the first time, confi
abstract: Majorana zero modes are quasiparticles that defy the usual quantum rulebook. Swap them around, and their collective state changes in ways that could power future computers. In a recent experiment, scientists created and braided these modes inside a Josephson device built from a topological insulator. They witnessed the telltale dance of in-gap states, exactly as predicted by the Fu–Kane model. This confirms that braiding operations—the heart of topological quantum computing—are actually feasible, bringing us closer to error-resistant machines.
threads: Квантовые частицы, которые помнят свой путь: учёные впервые увидели, как можно обменять местами загадочные «майорановские нули». Это может привести к созданию компьютеров, не боящихся ошибок.

title: Lunar Heating: How Tides Prolonged Earth's Magma Ocean
oneliner: Lunar tidal heat stretched the life of the molten rock ocean to half a billion years, altering Earth's early atmosphere.
description: After the impact that created the Moon, Earth became a liquid ball. Gravitational 'kneading' heated the planet, prolonging the magma ocean's life from 30 to 500 million years—depending on oxygen levels. Water vapor trapped heat, and weak tides enriched the air with hydrogen. This paints a picture of
abstract: After the Moon's birth, Earth was a magma ocean under a thick gaseous envelope. Scientists have for the first time accounted for the feedback between tidal heating from the nearby Moon and the redox state of the interior. It turned out that this coupling extends the lifetime of magma from 30 to 500 million years, and periods of planetary thermal balance last up to 320 million years. Interestingly, weak tides enriched the atmosphere with hydrogen sulfide and ammonia, creating a chemical imbalance—as if the ancient Earth was 'cooking' the air by a lunar recipe.
threads: Луна не просто висела в небе – её притяжение разогревало Землю изнутри, как гигантскую печь. Моделирование показало: это тепло могло поддерживать планету в расплавленном состоянии до 500 миллионов лет. Состав первичной атмосферы зависел от приливов и кислорода, оставляя загадочные следы в истории Земли.

title: Planetary pressure cooker: how gas and rock give birth to oceans
oneliner: Lab experiments recreated the interiors of rocky worlds and showed how hydrogen and molten rock produce water on the scale of tens of Earth's oceans.
description: Astronomers have found many rocky planets wrapped in a thick layer of hydrogen. Experiments with pressures of 600,000 atmospheres and heat above 4000°C have shown for the first time: deep inside, hydrogen pulls oxygen from rust (iron oxides) and turns it into water. This unseen chemical factory can
abstract: The most common type of planet in the Galaxy is a rocky body with a thick hydrogen atmosphere and a magma ocean. Models predicted that hydrogen would react with the melt, producing water, but experiments were lacking. For the first time, in diamond anvil cells at pressures of 16–60 GPa and temperatures above 4000 K, it has been shown: hydrogen dissolves actively in silicate melt, and its solubility depends more on temperature than on pressure. The reduction of iron oxide by hydrogen leads to the release of water and the formation of iron-rich melt droplets. It seems that water is generated in huge quantities inside such planets, altering the chemistry and structure of their depths and possibly the appearance of their atmospheres.
threads: Глубоко в недрах планет-скороварок перегретый водород сталкивается с пылающим камнем — и рождает воду. Новые эксперименты показали: такие «фабрики воды» могут скрываться внутри самых распространённых миров Галактики, переписывая историю их эволюции.

title: Magnetic Ripples: How Relativity Plays with Spins
oneliner: Scientists observed how electron spins in a new magnetic material form a wave pattern — a consequence of relativity theory.
description: A magnetic material has been discovered where electron spins arrange into ripples due to relativistic effects. This brings spintronics — spin-based electronics — closer to reality.
abstract: Alternagnetism is a new magnetic phase where electron spins alternate according to a wave law (d-, g-, or i-wave symmetry).
