It turns out that even a thousandfold enhancement of mixing does not lead to significant sme
abstract: In a new study, double-diffusive convection (where heat and stuff move at different speeds) has been baked into the first full evolutionary models of Jupiter and Saturn spanning 4.56 billion years. The verdict? The deep interior and the outer envelope barely mixed—less than an Earth's mass of heavy elements over their entire lives. Why? There's just not enough thermal energy to push material around, and unlike oversimplified simulations, the layers don't blend. This means these gas giants kept a "memory" of their formation, and their present makeup hints at violent events like mega-collisions.
threads: Океанографы давно знают: тёплые и солёные слои воды образуют ступеньки, не смешиваясь. Внутри Юпитера те же законы могли бы размыть ядро — но не смогли. Двойная диффузия, ленивый бармен планетных недр, потратила бы почти всю энергию планеты на то, чтобы поднять лишь пару земных масс. А значит, перемешивание задано рождением, и будущие наблюдения экзопланет увидят древние слои.

title: Cosmic Palimpsest: Gravitational Waves Reveal the Story of Ancestor Black Holes
oneliner: Events GW241011 and GW241110 turned out to be mergers involving second-generation black holes, confirming the hierarchical growth of these objects in the universe.
description: Analysis of two recent events from the GWTC-5.0 catalog showed that, with a probability 6–8 times higher than alternative explanations, the primary black holes in these systems were born in previous collisions. A Bayesian comparison of hypotheses favored the “2G+1G” scenario—a merger of a descendant
abstract: Astrophysicists studied two gravitational-wave events — GW241011 and GW241110 — in which black holes with highly unequal masses and rapid spin collided. Using Bayesian analysis, they compared the hypothesis of first-generation mergers (1G+1G) with hierarchical models (2G+1G), where one of the holes is a product of a previous merger, in two environments: star clusters and disks of active galactic nuclei. Both events confidently support the hierarchical scenario: the log Bayes factor was 6.5–8.6 for the first and 3.0–4.5 for the second. Models with accretion disks are slightly more likely due to the distribution of spin tilts, but the environment remains undetermined.
threads: Чёрная дыра может родиться дважды: сначала из умирающей звезды, а затем — из столкновения с другой. Каждый такой шаг оставляет след в её массе и вращении, словно строчки в космическом палимпсесте. Гравитационные волны снимают слой за слоем, открывая историю поколений. Возможно, именно так росли сверхмассивные дыры в центрах галактик, а часть тёмной материи — это древние чёрные дыры, прошедшие множество слияний.

title: The Universe's Dance Floor: When Dark Matter Breaks into a Quantum Rhythm
oneliner: A new theory shows how the competition between thermal chaos and quantum pressure in ultralight dark matter triggers a sharp regime of structure formation, explaining the mystery of dwarf galaxies.
description: Scientists have constructed a rigorous quantum-kinetic theory of gravitational instability for the fuzzy dark matter model—ultralight bosons with a mass on the order of 10⁻²² eV. By applying the Wigner equation and the Landau method, they derived a dispersion relation that revealed a sharp crossover
abstract: In the 'fuzzy' dark matter model, particles are so light that they exhibit quantum properties on cosmic scales. Scientists have constructed a kinetic theory that unifies gravitational instability and quantum effects, using an analog of the plasma dispersion function. They derived a key parameter — the ratio of quantum to thermal Jeans scales (α) — and discovered a sharp transition at α~0.5: at first, clumps are 'damped' by Landau damping (like a wave in plasma), and then by quantum pressure. This allows simultaneous estimation of the particle mass and their initial velocity dispersion from the shape of the matter spectrum — akin to judging the wind and pebbles on the bottom from ripples on a pond.
threads: Тёмная материя оказалась не просто холодным скелетом космоса, а квантовым танцполом, где тепловой шум борется с волнами-дирижёрами. Физики нашли точку смены ритма — параметр α около 0.5, когда квантовое давление резко перехватывает инициативу у случайных движений, словно невидимый диджей. Этот переход не только объяснит, почему карликовые галактики выглядят пушистыми, а не острыми, но и позволит, наблюдая за далёкими квазарами, взвесить частицы тёмной материи и измерить их ск

title: Cosmic Symphony: Algorithm Hears the Reverse Rhythm of the Early Universe
oneliner: Symbolic regression has detected unexpected inverse oscillations in the cosmic microwave background, hinting at an exotic phase of super-expansion after the Big Bang.
description: Analysis of data from the Planck satellite and ground-based telescopes using symbolic regression has revealed oscillations of the form cos(B/k) in the primordial perturbation spectrum. This inverse pattern is weakly preferred over standard alternatives and could be a remnant of a period of ultra-fas
abstract: In search of traces of new physics in the early Universe, astrophysicists study oscillations in the primordial power spectrum (the density distribution after the Big Bang). Using symbolic regression — an algorithm that independently derives formulas — they found that data from the Planck, ACT, and SPT telescopes are best described by a cos(B/k) pattern with B ≈ 4 Mpc⁻¹. This unconventional signal outperforms popular templates and faintly hints at a real phenomenon. This is how machine learning helps us listen to the Universe without bias.
threads: Алгоритм без подсказок разобрал реликтовое излучение по нотам и нашёл ритм, который не предсказывала ни одна теория. Это не простая волна: на больших масштабах она замедляется, как басовая струна космоса. Возможно, так отозвалась эпоха, где само пространство расширялось быстрее мыслимого, нарушая обычные законы. От этой находки до пульсирующих вселенных из фантастики — один научный шаг.

title: Stellar Nurseries in the Jet's Shadow: JWST Explores Centaurus A
oneliner: Using MIRI on JWST, astronomers glimpsed individual young stars in the heart of Centaurus A for the first time — 928 newborns hidden in dusty cocoons.
description: Centaurus A is the nearest radio galaxy to us, where an active supermassive black hole lives at its center, shooting out a powerful jet, while a warped dusty disk whirls around it. For a long time, it remained a mystery whether stars could be born under such extreme conditions. New observations with
abstract: The James Webb Space Telescope examined the central region of the nearby active galaxy Centaurus A. High-resolution images reveal that the 'oval dust shell' is actually a system of several twisted dust loops, bright in the mid-infrared. Color analysis uncovered 928 red point sources with a strong infrared excess — these are young stellar objects (YSOs), hidden in dust and pointing to star formation up to a million years old. These hotbeds align along the disk and are unrelated to the black hole’s jet. So, star birth was triggered by gas from a galactic merger, not by the core’s activity.
threads: В центре Центавра А, прямо под носом у сверхмассивной чёрной дыры, телескоп Уэбба насчитал 928 новорождённых звёзд, спрятанных в пылевом коконе. Это как обнаружить детский сад в цеху металлургического завода. Джет чёрной дыры оказался ни при чём — звезды рождает газ, принесённый древним слиянием. Заглянув в эти звёздные ясли, мы, возможно, поймём, как галактики тихо взрослеют, пока их ядра грохочут.

title: The Quantum Wave That Gives Birth to the Universe
oneliner: A scientist shows how a frozen quantum wave can hide curved spacetime within it.
description: A physicist found that interpreting a motionless quantum wave as a probability distribution for a particle’s internal clock naturally yields the math of an expanding universe. Gravity becomes unnecessary—spacetime bends purely from statistical chances. This offers a route to merging quantum physics
threads: Представьте: статичная квантовая волна, застывшая во времени, таит в себе целую вселенную. Если интерпретировать её амплитуду как распределение вероятности «внутреннего времени», из неё сама собой вырастает кривизна пространства-времени — безо всякой гравитации. Это значит, что тяготение может быть не фундаментальной силой, а тенью квантовых законов.

title: Symphony of Dark Energy: From Heavy Adagio to Explosive Allegro
oneliner: A new dark energy model with a smooth transition from attraction to repulsion reconciles measurements of the early and late Universe, resolving the Hubble constant tension.
description: In the standard cosmological model, two epochs of the Universe speak different languages: the temperature of the cosmic microwave background demands one expansion rate, while supernova explosions demand another. The discrepancy has surpassed the five-sigma threshold, becoming a true crisis. The ECDM
abstract: Cosmologists have developed a model in which dark energy changes sign: its density smoothly transitions from negative to positive. It’s like a pendulum that swung the other way. Calculations accounting for inhomogeneities were compared with data from Planck, DESI, Pantheon+ and others. The model agrees with observations and smooths out the Hubble tension.
threads: В споре о скорости расширения Вселенной долго не могли сойтись два хора: ранняя Вселенная пела в темпе 67, а поздняя — 73. Решение пришло оттуда, откуда не ждали: тёмная энергия, возможно, сменила настроение — от притяжения к отталкиванию. Плавный перелом, как смена adagio на allegro, сводит диссонанс к гармонии. Теперь телескопы проверят, не была ли эта модуляция лишь первой нотой в новом понимании космоса.

title: Waltz of Invisible Moons: The First Spectroscopic Find
oneliner: The radial velocity method has allowed us to hear the gravitational song of massive satellites around a brown dwarf, opening a new chapter in the search for exomoons.
description: Astronomers have discovered planetary-mass moons around a brown dwarf for the first time using high-precision spectroscopy. Observations with the VLT and the CRIRES+ spectrograph revealed periodic wobbles in the radial velocity of the starlike object CD-35 2722 B, hinting at at least one moon about
abstract: Of the 6000 known exoplanets, not a single exomoon (a moon orbiting a planet outside our solar system) has been confirmed. Now, compelling evidence has emerged for two moons orbiting the brown dwarf CD-35 2722 B. The radial velocity method was used: it detects subtle wobbles of the object caused by the gravitational pull of its moons. One candidate, a giant with a mass of at least 0.743 Jupiter masses, orbits every 169 days; the second, lighter one (0.277 Jupiter masses) completes an orbit in 87 days. Their orbits are in a 2:1 resonance, much like Jupiter's Galilean moons. The discovery promises a breakthrough in understanding how planetary systems form and in the search for habitable worlds.
threads: Коричневый карлик CD-35 B звучит как камертон: его спектральные линии слегка дрожат в такт гравитационным пинкам двух невидимых спутников. Масса главного — почти целый Юпитер. Эти луны тяжелее планет, и они кружатся в резонансе, как галилеевы спутники. В будущем мы, возможно, увидим их напрямую и даже поймаем гравитационные волны от их слияний. Космос снова оказался страннее фантастики.

title: The Ocean of Spacetime: The Hum of Black Holes and the Precision of Simulations
oneliner: Statistical analysis has shown that models of cosmic storms — black hole mergers — produce exactly the same background hum that pulsar beacons detect.
description: Recently, pulsar timing arrays have for the first time picked up the low-frequency hum of gravitational waves — a cosmic analog of the noise from distant storms in the ocean of spacetime. Comparing NANOGrav data with the Fable simulation showed that the predicted signal from supermassive black hole
abstract: Astronomers have developed the first statistical tool to assess discrepancies between data from pulsar timing arrays (PTA), which detect the gravitational wave background, and theoretical models of supermassive black hole mergers. Applying it to the FABLE simulation and NANOGrav data, they found that the tension does not exceed 2.5 sigma — that's not considered a serious contradiction. Interestingly, a slight increase in black hole masses in the early Universe or more mergers of equal-mass pairs almost completely eliminates the difference. This is the first step toward precisely 'tuning' our understanding of giant black hole growth using the gravitational 'hum' of the cosmos.
threads: Океан пространства-времени непрерывно штормит. Каждый шторм — слияние чёрных дыр — рождает гравитационные волны, которые спустя эпохи сливаются в низкочастотный гул. Словно маяки, пульсары улавливают эту рябь. Новые симуляции показали, что предсказания такого гула совпадают с наблюдениями. Мы учимся слушать дыхание Вселенной — и это только начало.

title: Catching the Neutrino Wind: Why the Faintest Breeze in the Universe Demands the Impossible
oneliner: Physicists have determined: to feel the 'neutrino wind', you need an exposure 100,000 times greater than for simply detecting relic neutrinos.
description: The cosmic neutrino background is a ghostly echo of the Big Bang, billions of neutrinos permeating every cubic centimeter of space. Scientists have been hunting for its direct detection for years, but new work shows that measuring its anisotropy—the 'neutrino wind'—is a task orders of magnitude hard
abstract: Scientists are keen to detect relic neutrinos (particles born in the first moment of the Universe) and study their “wind”—the anisotropy (unevenness) of the flow caused by Earth’s motion. Analysis of electron recoils in tritium decay shows: to notice this wind, you need 100,000 times more data than simply detecting them. If instrument precision exceeds a certain threshold, all disturbances must be perfectly controlled, and for Majorana neutrinos the signal is further weakened. It’s like trying to hear a whisper in a hurricane.
threads: Наша планета несётся сквозь реликтовый нейтринный газ со скоростью 370 км/с, но мы этого не замечаем — ветер слишком призрачен. Чтобы поймать его направление, нужна экспозиция в 100 000 раз больше, чем для обнаружения самого фона. Это как пытаться услышать, с какой стороны дует самый слабый сквозняк во Вселенной, находясь внутри урагана. Но если мы научимся, то сможем прочесть летопись первых секунд мироздания и понять, почему материя победила антиматерию.

title: The Pulse of a Black Hole: Birth of a Jet in X-ray Echo
oneliner: In the galaxy 1ES 1927+654, astronomers caught the emergence of X-ray emission lines as the black hole launched a relativistic jet, like a mute instrument suddenly striking up a tune.
description: Years of observations with XMM-Newton, Swift, and VLA showed how the changing-look active galaxy 1ES 1927+654 underwent a dramatic transition from a wind regime to a jet. X-ray spectra revealed oxygen emission lines and a broad iron line, while ionized absorption faded. Synchronously, radio emission
abstract: Astronomers tracked the birth of a jet in the active galactic nucleus of 1ES 1927+654 using X-ray, optical, and radio telescopes. They watched as powerful outflows (winds) weakened, and energy was channeled into a narrow relativistic beam. For the first time, emission lines appeared in the spectrum, including a rare iron line, signaling a restructuring of the inner accretion disk. Curiously, after a few years, the system stabilized: X-ray and radio fluxes plateaued, as if a cosmic engine shifted gears.
threads: Чёрная дыра в галактике 1ES 1927+654 переключилась с ветров на джет. Её пульс — QPO — участился с 0.9 до 2.5 мГц, как сердце при старте. Спектр вспыхнул эмиссионными линиями, а джет разогнался до 0.2c. Это переход, видимый в реальном времени, приоткрывает механизм запуска релятивистских струй и их роль в эволюции галактик.

title: Magnetic Echolocation: Measuring the Cosmic Cocoons of Fast Radio Bursts
oneliner: A combination of three radio wave propagation effects allows us to estimate the distance to turbulent plasma around repeating fast radio bursts.
description: Fast radio bursts (FRBs) — millisecond signals of colossal energy — remain one of the great mysteries of astrophysics. Their environment is often saturated with magnetized plasma that distorts polarization and scatters the pulses. A new technique combines the analysis of these distortions, akin to d
abstract: Fast radio bursts (FRBs) are powerful millisecond radio pulses of unknown nature. The environment around repeating sources often has a strong magnetic field, which distinguishes it from ordinary interstellar matter. The authors developed a method to estimate the physical size of this environment using three observed effects: pulse scattering, loss of polarization, and changes in Faraday rotation measure. The analysis showed that for FRB 20190303A, 20190417A, and 20190520B, the perturbed region is most likely comparable to a supernova remnant, while for FRB 20180916B and 20201124A, a binary system scale cannot be ruled out.
threads: Среди радиосигналов из глубин космоса прячутся маяки, чьи сигналы искажаются магнитными щупальцами плазмы. Их танец рассказывает о расстояниях в парсеки и астрономические единицы — как если бы мы учились различать эхо в горном ущелье и гул в пещере. Вскоре мы сможем читать историю звёздных смертей по мерцанию этих всплесков.

title: Symphony of Shadows: Neutrino Telescopes Catch the Whisper of Dark Matter
oneliner: Analysis of data from neutrino observatories opens a new window into the search for light dark matter particles arriving from the center of our Galaxy.
description: Using 13 years of data from the ANTARES telescope, scientists have set stringent limits on the interaction of dark matter with ordinary matter across mass scales from keV to GeV. Collisions of cosmic rays with dark matter particles in the Galactic Ridge produce neutrinos that are detected by deep-se
abstract: IceCube and ANTARES recently spotted a surplus of high-energy neutrinos streaming from the galactic plane. One idea: cosmic rays bouncing off lightweight dark matter (sub-GeV) give birth to neutrinos as mesons break apart. By mapping cosmic rays and using ANTARES data, scientists set upper bounds on how strongly dark matter interacts with ordinary matter for masses down to keV. So neutrino telescopes are now a fresh tool for hunting light dark matter, and upcoming detectors like IceCube-Gen2 and KM3NeT will sharpen the search.
threads: Тёмная материя невидима, но её следы — как тихие аккорды в симфонии космических лучей. Нейтринные телескопы, погружённые в бездну океана, ловят эти ноты из центра Галактики. С новыми обсерваториями, возможно, мы впервые услышим голос скрытой Вселенной, который изменит наше понимание мироустройства.

title: The Forge of Black Holes: Stellar Mergers as a Source of Spin
oneliner: In densely populated star clusters, mergers of massive stars before collapse impart rapid spin to newborn black holes, explaining data from gravitational-wave observatories.
description: In dense globular clusters, frequent collisions of massive stars lead to the formation of rapidly rotating blue supergiants. Simulations show that when the mass ratio of the components exceeds 0.3, the merger product does not expand into a red supergiant and retains its angular momentum. The collaps
abstract: Contrary to expectations that black holes are born spinning slowly, modeling shows that in star clusters, mergers of massive stars can spin them up significantly. About half of black holes in such systems originate from mergers, and up to 10% from major mergers (with a mass ratio greater than 1:10). In some cases, the dimensionless spin parameter reaches 0.5 or more — like a spinning top given a sharp flick. This changes our understanding of binary black hole formation and the detection of gravitational waves from them.
threads: В тесных звёздных скоплениях идёт невидимая кузнечная работа: звёзды сталкиваются, сливаются, и их танец закручивает будущие чёрные дыры до бешеных скоростей. Одно слияние — и безликая дыра обретает спин, способный вышвырнуть её из скопления. Именно такие объекты ловят детекторы гравитационных волн. Возможно, это ключ к загадке длинных гамма-всплесков.

title: The Mystery of Fragile Satellites: Dark Matter Under Tidal Assault
oneliner: Tidal disruption of the Milky Way’s dwarf satellite galaxies casts doubt on dark matter and points toward modified gravity.
description: Elena Asencio and her colleagues showed that, contrary to standard cosmology, the Milky Way’s classical satellites suffer strong tidal deformations. In MOND (modified Newtonian dynamics) these disruptions are natural, while cold dark matter predicts they should be almost completely shielded by massi
abstract: Classical satellites of the Milky Way often have tidal tails and distortions, even though in the standard cosmological model, massive dark matter halos should make them stable. Researchers compared the half-mass radius (a size indicator) with the theoretical tidal radius (the boundary where the Galaxy's gravity overpowers) at pericenter, for two models — standard and MOND. It turned out that in MOND, almost all satellites should experience tidal effects, which agrees with observations. Moreover, tidal forces can heat stars inside the satellite, increasing velocity dispersion — this also explains the puzzlingly high velocity dispersions known for some satellites.
threads: Астрономы заметили, что спутники Млечного Пути вытягиваются и рассыпаются, словно их разрывает невидимый прилив. В стандартной космологии это невозможно — там гало тёмной материи служат щитом. Но числа показывают обратное: приливная восприимчивость многих карликов превышает порог разрушения. Это как увидеть трещины на броне космического корабля. Всё больше учёных обращаются к MOND — теории, где гравитация в слабых полях становится сильнее. Отказ от тёмной материи сулит перево

title: The Arrow Piercing the Disk: Recipe for an X-ray Flare
oneliner: The speed of the cosmic arrow and the density of the target dictate the brightness and duration of flares near the event horizon of a black hole.
description: When a wandering star slams into the accretion disk of a supermassive black hole at immense speed, a quasi-periodic X-ray flare is born — a cosmic firework capable of outshining a galaxy. For the first time, 3D radiation hydrodynamics simulations systematically linked the collision parameters — star
abstract: Astrophysicists simulated collisions of a star with the disk around a supermassive black hole to explain quasi-periodic eruptions (QPEs) — repeating X-ray flares. Calculations showed: the faster and larger the star, the brighter the flare; a dense disk lengthens it, while vertical concentration of the disk makes the flare shorter and brighter. Oblique impacts smooth out the asymmetry of double peaks. For the source GSN 069, the best model is a Sun-sized star moving against the rotation of a dense disk, leftover from the destruction of another star. The derived patterns allow us to piece together QPE characteristics like a puzzle from system parameters.
threads: Когда звезда-стрела на скорости 0.1c врезается в диск чёрной дыры, рождается вспышка, на миг затмевающая галактику. Моделирование показало: яркость растёт как квадрат скорости, а длительность — как корень из плотности диска. Это космический лучник: каждый выстрел оставляет световой след. Будущие гравитационно-волновые антенны уловят эти выстрелы, раскрывая тайны горизонта событий.

title: Globular Clusters: A Palimpsest of Ancient Gas Clouds
oneliner: The chemical patterns in globular clusters are not the product of stellar evolution but a legacy from inhomogeneous molecular clouds that preserve a memory of turbulent events in the early galaxy.
description: For decades astronomers puzzled over mysterious anti-correlations of light elements in globular clusters. New research based on high-resolution cosmological simulations shows: the chemical anomalies could have arisen long before the birth of the cluster's first stars—in giant gas clouds. This scenar
abstract: Chemical variations in globular clusters were traditionally explained by self-enrichment—ejecta from the first stars. New cosmological simulations reveal a different path: gas clouds, colliding after a burst of star formation, already contain the needed pattern—an anti-correlation of nitrogen and oxygen with unchanged iron. Such clouds become the cradle of clusters, passing on chemistry 'by inheritance'. It's like a ready-made sauce where all the spices are mixed before it hits the table. The discovery turns globular clusters into a chronicle of gas processes in the early Universe.
threads: Звёздные химики десятилетиями ломали голову: звёзды в шаровых скоплениях словно состояли из двух разных популяций, хотя родились одновременно. Новое исследование показало: ответ скрыт в газовых облаках-предшественниках, которые, как многослойный палимпсест, записали историю галактики ещё до рождения скопления. Возможно, так же рождались и первые галактики. Мы только учимся читать эти древние тексты.

title: Flattened Darkness: Stellar Streams Outline Invisible Halos
oneliner: Analysis of 32 extragalactic stellar streams shows that dark matter halos around nearby galaxies are on average squashed like pumpkins.
description: Using the STRRINGS catalog of 32 stellar streams, astronomers have for the first time measured the population distribution of dark matter halo shapes beyond the Local Group. Applying Bayesian analysis, they found that for the 17 most reliable streams, the median flattening is about 0.72 — meaning th
abstract: Using stellar streams—tidal tails of dwarf galaxies—scientists have for the first time measured the shape of dark matter beyond our Galaxy. Applying Bayesian analysis to 32 streams, they selected 17 "golden" objects with clear trajectories and determined that, on average, halos are flattened with a compression parameter of 0.72. This agrees with cosmological simulations. The result lays the groundwork for future large-scale surveys with the Euclid and LSST telescopes.
threads: Звёздные хвосты галактик, словно ленты танцоров, очертили форму тёмных гало. Оказалось, невидимая материя не сферична — она сплюснута, как тыква. Анализ 32 потоков показал, что среднее сжатие около 0,72, и это идеально совпадает с космологическими симуляциями. Вскоре обзоры вроде Euclid поставят картографирование скрытой Вселенной на поток.

title: The Icy Moons' Double Breath: JWST Unravels the Mystery of Uranus's Carbon Dioxide
oneliner: JWST data have, for the first time, reliably separated radiolytic and endogenous sources of carbon dioxide on four of Uranus's icy moons.
description: Spectrograph NIRSpec aboard the JWST telescope has discerned a complex mosaic of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide bands on Ariel, Umbriel, Titania, and Oberon. Comparison with laboratory ices at cryogenic temperatures revealed signatures of crystalline CO₂, clathrates, and carbonates. The observed
abstract: Using the NIRSpec infrared spectrometer aboard the James Webb Space Telescope, astronomers analyzed the composition of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide on Uranus’s four largest moons. Spectral lines indicated that on Ariel and Umbriel these gases are concentrated on their trailing hemispheres, suggesting a radiolytic origin (from charged particle bombardment). However, part of the spectrum, such as faint bands from carbonates and clathrates (gases trapped in ice crystal lattices), hints that gas could be seeping from the interior. Carbon dioxide turned out to be widespread across the Uranian system—in the rings and small moons.
threads: Холодные луны Урана кажутся замороженными, но их поверхность — арена химических превращений. JWST уловил двойной источник углекислоты: внешний радиолиз и дыхание недр. Как два дирижёра в оркестре. Когда-нибудь зонд сядет на Ариэль и прочтёт летопись Солнечной системы в слоях этого льда.

title: Icy Whisper: O-PTIR Captures Amino Acids Beyond Diffraction
oneliner: Optical-photothermal spectroscopy detects single amino acid molecules in water ice without damaging the sample—a step toward finding life on the moons of Jupiter and Saturn.
description: The O-PTIR method shatters the diffraction barrier: infrared heating translates into thermal expansion, read by a visible laser. This enables submicron resolution and sensitivity down to 0.2 µM. Crucially, organics distort the ice's own absorption bands, creating a subtle yet informative spectral fo
abstract: Researchers used the O-PTIR method (optical photothermal infrared spectroscopy) for non-destructive detection of organics in water ice with submicron resolution. They managed to detect the amino acid L-glycine at concentrations down to parts per million and even distinguish between two different substances in one sample. Curiously, the presence of organics appeared in the spectrum as a broad signal near the water bending band, absent in pure H₂O and D₂O. Such details will help more accurately interpret data on the composition of ices on moons and comets.
threads: Аминокислоты во льду не поют сольно — они искажают общий хор молекул воды. O-PTIR улавливает это тепловое эхо с чувствительностью в одну таблетку аспирина на олимпийский бассейн. Возможно, именно так мы найдём жизнь на Энцеладе: не по прямому сигналу, а по тому, как лёд звучит иначе.

title: Quantum Mpemba effect: hot cools faster, but only near absolute zero
oneliner: Quantum paradox: hot cools down faster than cold, but only near absolute zero.
description: The Mpemba effect, known from kitchen experiments with water, is reimagined in a quantum model. It turns out that at the level of individual particles, hot cools faster only at temperatures close to absolute zero, where quantum tunneling operates. Reverse paradoxes also emerge, unthinkable in the or
abstract: The Mpemba effect — when hot freezes faster than warm. Scientists studied its quantum version in a model with two wells (bistable potential). Quantization shifted the effect to ultralow temperatures, orders of magnitude colder than the classical case, and caused inverse and double inverse effects — as if not only the hot outpaced the warm, but the freezing order flipped. This shows that quantum effects can radically change even canonical non-equilibrium phenomena.
threads: Горячая вода замерзает быстрее? Этот загадочный эффект заиграл новыми красками в квантовом мире. Учёные показали, что при температуре, близкой к абсолютному нулю, горячее превращается в холодное быстрее благодаря квантовым скачкам. И даже наоборот — холодное может обгонять горячее. Квантовая магия тепла.

title: An Ultrafast Look at Light’s Quantum Quiver
oneliner: Scientists 'photographed' the quantum ripples of bright light for the first time, using record-breaking ultra-short flashes.
description: Light constantly trembles due to quantum effects, but measuring this tremor in bright beams was impossible. Physicists used flashes shorter than the ripples themselves and reconstructed their pattern for the first time. This will simplify the creation of precise quantum devices.
abstract: Attosecond metrology measures light wave oscillations on ultrafast timescales, but previously it only worked with classical light. Now, scientists have shown that the attosecond streaking method is sensitive to quantum fluctuations of bright light, making it possible to extract squeezing properties where conventional tomography fails. The developed scheme measures quantum squeezing below the shot-noise level, paving the way toward attosecond metrology of quantum fluctuations. It's like hearing the whisper of the vacuum with unprecedented clarity.
threads: Свет дрожит — и не просто так: в его квантовой ряби спрятаны секреты, которые раньше ускользали от приборов. Теперь учёные смогли «сфотографировать» эту дрожь с помощью вспышек короче, чем колебание самого света. Это как услышать шёпот в грохоте — и открыть дверь к сверхточным квантовым технологиям.

title: Classical Gravity Distorts Quantum Snapshots
oneliner: A new method distinguishes quantum gravity from classical by anomalies in measurements.
description: Scientists have proposed a way to test the nature of gravity by observing the oscillations of microscopic mirrors. If gravity is classical, the reconstructed quantum picture violates fundamental constraints—opening a path to lab tests.
abstract: The authors studied how classical gravity (the Schrödinger-Newton theory) affects the tomography of a massive quantum oscillator. State-dependent corrections arise: the reconstructed covariance matrix becomes sensitive to observation angles and can even exceed the bounds set by the uncertainty principle. The difference from the quantum model was assessed using the Hellinger distance, revealing its dependence on temperature and measurement strength. Essentially, this is nonlinear quantum mechanics: the measurement process alters itself.
threads: Оказывается, если гравитация классическая, то при попытке сделать квантовый «снимок» зеркальца результат вылезает за рамки, запрещённые принципом неопределённости. Это как камера, которая трясётся в такт с тем, что снимает — учёные учатся ловить такие искажения.

title: Atoms Get Entangled in Space: A New Kind of Quantum Link
oneliner: Scientists have for the first time entangled the positions of two atoms separated by hundreds of microns.
description: Using Rydberg blockade, physicists linked the motion of two atoms in space: when one received a light push and moved, the other stayed put. This quantum connection opens a path to complex quantum systems for computing and simulation.
abstract: Physicists used Rydberg blockade (a phenomenon where excitation of one atom prevents excitation of a neighbor) and photon recoil (a laser kick) to entangle the positions of two neutral atoms. When one atom shifts due to the kick, the other, thanks to the blockade, stays put, but their coordinates become correlated. After trapping, a Bell state emerges where each atom has some probability of being in one of two locations, separated by up to hundreds of microns. This result is remarkable because macroscopic separation of entangled particles opens new possibilities for quantum technologies.
threads: Два атома играют в квантовую чехарду: один получает световой пинок и отскакивает на сотни микрон, но только если второй разрешит. Так рождается запутанность положений — новый трюк для квантовых компьютеров будущего.

title: The Search for Extraterrestrial Life: From Smoke to Gardens
oneliner: Scientists suggest looking for green alien technologies, not industrial smog.
description: We've been searching the skies for factory chimney smoke, but we should be looking for blooming gardens. A new approach in astronomy suggests shifting focus from pollution to ecological harmony.
abstract: Traditionally, technosignatures are about searching for 'pollution': excess heat or industrial gases. This approach, as the authors note following indigenous scientists, likens extraterrestrial intelligence to colonial expansion: we look for those who transform the environment, not those who live in harmony with it. The work suggests searching for technosignatures that sustain nature — a kind of 'eco-friendly' trace of technology, converging with biosignatures. This is an unexpected angle: perhaps we haven't found other civilizations precisely because we're looking for the wrong signs.
threads: Поиск инопланетян по загрязнению похож на поиск колонизаторов. Разумные цивилизации могут жить в гармонии с природой, не оставляя грязных следов. Так можно ответить на парадокс Ферми.

title: Noise Gives Birth to Quantum Order
oneliner: Noise, which usually destroys everything in its path, can line up particles into a perfect one-way wave.
description: Physicists have discovered that coordinated noise creates a stable pattern of particles moving strictly in one direction. It resembles a stadium wave frozen in one direction. The finding overturns the idea that interference always ruins quantum effects: noise can be the architect of order.
abstract: Usually decoherence (the destruction of quantum states by the environment) is considered the enemy of quantum devices, but researchers have shown that it can induce topological phenomena. In a model with phase noise, where noise events are correlated (interconnected), asymmetric diffusion of particles arises—their directional drift, determined by a topological invariant (winding number). This effect is purely interactional, disappears when measurement outcomes are postselected, and thus is a true manifestation of an open quantum system, without classical analog. Just as the wind shapes dunes, quantum noise sculpts robust topological order.
threads: Что, если шум не враг, а создатель? Учёные показали, что квантовый беспорядок может выстроить частицы в одностороннюю волну — словно стадионная «мексиканская волна» зафиксирована в одном направлении. Так рождается топологическая материя.

title: Why Nebulae Fool Astronomers
oneliner: When measuring gas density in nebulae using different methods, astronomers get different numbers — this isn't a mistake, but a key to understanding stellar nurseries.
description: Astronomers have discovered that gas density in nebulae can't be described by a single number. Each measurement method only responds to the density range it is sensitive to. This resolves a long-standing contradiction and calls for recalculating masses and energies in star-forming regions.
abstract: Astronomers measure the density of ionized gas using spectral lines, but different methods yield results that differ by hundreds of times. It was believed that the issue was due to inaccurate atomic data or different depths of line formation. However, it turned out that each method is sensitive to its own density range, and real nebulae contain gas with densities ranging from very low to very high. Just as rulers of different lengths only show the depth at individual points of a river, the lines 'see' only gas of a certain density. This discovery resolves a long-standing puzzle and forces a reevaluation of mass, pressure, and gas composition estimates everywhere—from nearby regions to distant galaxies.
threads: Взгляните на фото туманности — это не просто пушистое облако, а смесь разреженного газа и плотных комков. Каждый прибор по-своему определяет плотность, и это не ошибка, а ключ к пониманию звёздных яслей. Плотность там — не одно число, а целый спектр значений.

title: Dark Energy's Seesaw: A New Explanation for a Strange Signal
oneliner: Dark energy fluctuations explained without new physics: particles within it temporarily cluster, and galaxy clusters amplify this, causing sharp surges in cosmic expansion.
description: The mysterious force accelerating the universe turns out to be fickle. In a new model, dark energy is like steam that partially condenses into 'droplets' when cooled. Massive galaxy clusters act as refrigerators, their gravity creating pressure differences. This solves recent anomalies in supernova
abstract: Recent data from DESI, the cosmic microwave background, and supernovae hint that dark energy may have crossed the 'phantom barrier' (w = –1). The authors explain this by the backreaction — the influence of matter clumps on the expansion of the Universe — and the dynamics of a quintom field. As a result, the barrier is crossed twice: first downward, then upward. The model agrees with observations and gives a transition at z≈0.35. Thus, the gravitational 'noise' from galaxy clusters can look like an exotic substance.
threads: Вселенная расширяется с ускорением из-за загадочной тёмной энергии. Но недавние наблюдения показали: её «напор» словно колебался — сначала усилился, потом ослабел. Новое объяснение, использующее физику конденсации частиц и неравномерности космоса, обходится без экзотических полей.

title: Dark Matter on Two Notes
oneliner: Ultralight dark matter leaves a double imprint in gravitational waves—the future LISA observatory will be able to catch it.
description: Nearly weightless dark matter leaves a dual imprint on the fabric of space. Thanks to quadratic interaction, two types of ripples emerge that the space detector LISA will catch. Unlike ground-based instruments, LISA is immune to noise, offering a chance to detect the elusive substance directly for t
abstract: Ultralight dark matter could interact with ordinary particles beyond gravity — for instance, quadratically (with a force proportional to the square of the field). In the future space-based gravitational-wave detector LISA, this would produce signals at two distinct frequencies: one matching twice the mass of the dark matter particles, the other at the ultra-low frequencies of their thermal motion. Calculations show that LISA will outperform all current limits, including astrophysical ones, across several mass ranges. Its location in space eliminates the shielding that plagues Earth-based instruments — like suddenly hearing a whisper in the open cosmos rather than through a wall.
threads: Тёмная материя может 'звучать' на двух нотах одновременно. LISA услышит этот дуэт и раскроет секреты невидимого вещества.

title: Quantum Engine Combines Power with Ideal Efficiency
oneliner: Physicists have proposed a superconducting circuit where multiple quantum systems, working together as a single coordinated mechanism, almost reach the theoretical maximum of efficiency without losing power.
description: Ordinary heat engines can't be both powerful and maximally efficient: upping power increases losses. New research describes a quantum engine on a superconducting chip, where many quantum systems synchronize like fireflies in a forest, bypassing the classic trade-off. This paves the way to energy-sav
abstract: Classical heat engines face a trade-off between power and efficiency: you can't run at full power and achieve Carnot efficiency at the same time. In quantum systems, however, collective quantum jumps—where particles transition between states in unison—can overcome this limit. Scientists have proposed the first concrete implementation of such an engine using superconducting circuits, a platform already familiar from quantum computing. The device mimics collective enhancement, allowing it to asymptotically approach Carnot efficiency at finite power, like a well-coordinated rowing team where each stroke amplifies the overall motion without extra effort.
threads: Обычные двигатели: больше мощности — ниже КПД. Квантовый трюк с коллективным усилением ломает это правило. Сверхпроводящий чип обещает моторы, почти идеальные и при этом мощные. Шаг к энергетике без потерь.

title: The Safe at the Edge of the Universe
oneliner: Like a safe with a timer, the cosmological horizon doesn't release information immediately. Physicists have calculated the exact moment when this happens.
description: Using a simplified model, researchers determined the delay with which information emerges from behind the cosmological horizon. Quantum particles born near the horizon carry data not instantly: first, a characteristic time passes — roughly 1/8 of the horizon’s traversal period. This discovery helps
abstract: The question of when information leaves the cosmological horizon is akin to the black hole information paradox. In a new study, a Hawking pair of quanta was modeled in a two-dimensional spacetime smoothly connecting AdS2 and dS2. Using the algebraic crossed product formalism, the entropy difference was computed, yielding a mini-Page curve: its minimum occurs at a time of about τ≈β/8, indicating the start of information leakage. The relative modular flow gives a Lyapunov exponent λ=2π/β — the scrambling time after which an observer in the static patch can read out the data. The discovery resembles a 'boiling' effect: at first the process is imperceptible, then a key moment arrives.
threads: Представьте дым над чашкой кофе: каждая струйка уносит информацию. Физики выяснили, в какой момент информация начинает утекать из космологического горизонта — как в испаряющейся чёрной дыре. Расчёт показал: это происходит через 1/8 характерного времени после рождения пары частиц.

title: Свет, который переключают быстрее собственных колебаний
oneliner: Физики переключают «сжатие» света за миллиардные доли миллиардной доли секунды.
description: Ученые создали способ переключать особое состояние света — «сжатый» свет — за невообразимо короткое время. Если обычный свет дрожит хаотично, то здесь шум перераспределяют, словно сжимая воздушный шар. Теперь можно менять, какую часть шума подавлять, практически мгновенно, с помощью пары лазерных им
abstract: Squeezed light reduces quantum noise, making measurements super-precise. Physicists have learned to switch the type of squeezing (amplitude ↔ phase) within fractions of a light wave period by controlling the nonlinear response of the medium with powerful femtosecond pulses. This is done by simply shifting the phase between them — like tuning a radio dial to catch a different station. Frequency-resolved measurements revealed quantum correlations between modes. This result paves the way for tunable sources of quantum light and 'instant' diagnostics of quantum materials.

title: Жизнь растений продлится дольше миллиарда лет
oneliner: Новое моделирование показывает, что земные растения могут просуществовать более 1,6 миллиарда лет, несмотря на усиление солнечного нагрева.
description: Работа оценивает, как долго ещё сохранится растительная жизнь на Земле по мере того, как Солнце становится ярче. Учтены влияние выветривания морского дна и случайные изменения выбросов CO₂. Исследование важно для понимания будущего нашей биосферы и поиска жизни на других планетах.
abstract: The Sun is gradually getting brighter, and someday this will make Earth uninhabitable for plants—either due to overheating or lack of carbon dioxide. Previous estimates gave the biosphere about a billion more years, but a recent study extended that to 1.6–1.86 billion years by more accurately accounting for rock weathering (the process that binds CO₂). The new model includes seafloor weathering and random fluctuations in CO₂ emissions from the Earth's interior. It turned out that if seafloor weathering plays a major role, the timeline could shrink, but it would still be over a billion years. It's like a race between two 'chemical pumps'—continental and oceanic—and the fate of green life hangs in their balance.

title: Световое эхо переворачивает химию
oneliner: В ловушке из зеркал световое эхо перекраивает молекулы, заставляя их вырабатывать магнитные поля и кольцевые токи.
description: В оптической полости световое эхо меняет правила игры: водород теряет стабильность, а кольцевые молекулы выпрямляются и запускают безостановочный ток. Эффект нарастает с числом молекул, открывая путь к управлению химией светом.
abstract: Using high-precision quantum calculations, researchers studied how the interaction of molecules with a magnetic field inside an optical resonator (cavity) changes their properties. It turned out that strong coupling with the field makes the ground state of the hydrogen molecule metastable (temporary) and reverses the energy order of electron pairs. In ring-shaped molecules prone to distortions (the Jahn-Teller effect: spontaneous deformation to lower energy), the field acts as a magnetic "corset", preserving the symmetric shape and creating unusual antiaromatic states with polarized spins or ring currents. The effect intensifies with molecular concentration, opening prospects for controlling chemistry in the regime of deep resonator–matter coupling.

title: Ритм чёрных дыр из ранней Вселенной
oneliner: Рождение первичных чёрных дыр подчиняется ритму, где один и тот же скачок массы повторяется снова и снова, словно эхо.
description: Учёные выяснили, что в расширяющейся Вселенной первичные чёрные дыры рождаются ритмично: их масса растёт не плавно, а скачками, образуя повторяющийся узор. Раньше такое поведение наблюдали лишь в моделях без расширения. Открытие подсказывает, что в распределении масс этих дыр должны быть заметные пи
abstract: Scientists simulated the formation of primordial black holes in an expanding universe and discovered that their masses oscillate in a log-periodic pattern—a signature of discrete self-similarity (where a system repeats its behavior at smaller scales in a step-like manner). Unlike previous studies in a static universe, here the oscillations between peaks and troughs are more uneven. This "cosmic staircase" in black hole masses could imprint subtle modulations on gravitational wave backgrounds, offering a new way to probe the universe's infancy.

title: Магнитные «наушники» для кубитов: новый способ борьбы с квантовым шумом
oneliner: Учёные создали сверхтонкие магнитные узоры, которые, подобно шумоподавляющим наушникам, защищают квантовые биты от губительного магнитного фона.
description: Квантовые состояния легко разрушаются под действием магнитных помех. Обычные методы настройки частоты здесь бессильны, но исследователи применили иной подход: сверхкомпактные магнитные узоры, расположенные вплотную к искусственным атомам в алмазе (NV-центрам), поглощают низкочастотный шум. Экспериме
abstract: Nanophotonic structures can be used to control dephasing—a process that destroys quantum information due to noise. Researchers created metasurfaces from a magnetic alloy CoFeB and suppressed low-frequency magnetic fluctuations (~MHz) in NV centers in diamond. Unlike the Purcell effect (which accelerates emission), here the emission rate wasn't changed, but the nature of dephasing was. These structures act like a filter that selectively blocks magnetic noise at certain frequencies. This result paves the way for more stable qubits without complex isolation.

title: Вселенная без тёмной энергии? Простая модель обходит стандартную
oneliner: Модель $R_h=ct$ обходится без тёмной энергии и соблюдает все правила гравитации.
description: Сравнение двух моделей: стандартная требует невидимую тёмную энергию, альтернативная играет честно. Данные с вероятностью 92% поддерживают простой вариант.
abstract: In general relativity, four energy conditions act as strict prohibitions against negative energy, gravitational repulsion, and superluminal flows. Violating them requires exotic matter, but the standard ΛCDM model does this constantly: during inflation and in the dark energy era. Analysis of HII galaxies and cosmic chronometers shows that the alternative R_h=ct model not only fits observations significantly better (92% probability vs 8%) but also fully satisfies all four conditions, whereas ΛCDM violates the strong energy condition at low redshifts (z<2).

title: Свет притворяется фермионом при ускорении
oneliner: При особой траектории ускоренный заряд излучает свет, который распределяется по энергиям как частицы-фермионы.
description: Если электрический заряд движется по хитрой кривой, его излучение начинает вести себя необычно: фотоны занимают строго отдельные энергетические уровни, будто проходят через турникет. Этот эффект стирает границы между бозонами и фермионами и открывает новые возможности для квантовых технологий.
abstract: A rapidly moving charge can generate radiation whose spectrum mimics the Fermi-Dirac distribution (like that of fermions, where the Pauli exclusion principle applies). This is surprising because photons are bosons, which have no such restrictions. The effect arises from the special kinematics of acceleration, without any connection to thermal equilibrium or horizons. Imagine a flashlight on a carousel: its light paints a pattern of individual flashes, even though the beam is continuous.

title: Математика закрывает путь квантовому компьютеру на частицах-двойниках
oneliner: Математическое доказательство лишает физиков надежды на частицы-двойники в сверхпроводниках — ключ к защищённому квантовому компьютеру.
description: Учёные строго доказали, что особые состояния в сверхпроводниках — не загадочные частицы, тождественные своим античастицам, а обычные электронные сгустки с зарядом. Это перечёркивает идею использовать их для надёжных квантовых вычислений через «плетение». Теперь физикам, возможно, придётся пересмотре
abstract: The authors have rigorously proven that in systems where particle number is conserved, quasiparticles carry the same charge and mass as ordinary particles. This means that Bogoliubov zero modes (excitations in superconductors) cannot be their own antiparticles and are unsuitable for quantum braiding operations. This casts doubt on years of effort to build a quantum computer based on Majorana modes. Moreover, the work shows how hard it is to prepare the necessary quantum states using slowly varying external fields. The bottom line: it’s time to go back to developing a theory of superconductivity without artificially breaking particle-number symmetry.

title: Гигантский шум в сэндвиче из сверхпроводника и магнита
oneliner: Электрический шум в сэндвиче из сверхпроводника, изолятора и магнита оказался в сотни раз больше ожидаемого, указав на рождение нового вида сверхпроводимости.
description: В наносэндвиче из ванадия, оксида магния и железа электрический шум усилился в сотни раз. Причина: магнитное железо на время стало сверхпроводником — в нём возникла сверхпроводимость, где электронные пары движутся с параллельными спинами. Раньше для такого эффекта требовались два сверхпроводника, зд
abstract: By measuring shot noise (a consequence of charge quantization) in superconductor/insulator/ferromagnet structures (V/MgO/Fe), the authors captured its giant surge—orders of magnitude above the calculated value. The culprit? An exotic proximity effect: the superconductor nudges neighboring layers, and thanks to spin-orbit coupling (the interplay between spin and motion), triplet superconductivity blossoms there, radically different from the original. It’s like a single word flipping the meaning of an entire phrase. Such amplified noise was previously spotted only in junctions with two superconductors, never in a solo one—this discovery forces us to rethink the role of spin-orbit effects.

title: Как лазер поможет различать воду и лёд в облаках
oneliner: Специальный квантовый фильтр научит спутниковые лазеры безошибочно отличать воду от льда в облаках.
description: Обычные спутниковые лазеры не различают воду и лёд в облаках из-за ярких бликов. Квантовый фильтр выделяет этот блеск, превращая помеху в данные, и позволяет точно определить, из чего состоит облако.
abstract: Specular reflections hinder space lidars from determining whether clouds are made of water or ice. A quantum lidar with parametric mode separation (QPMS) has been proposed, which, thanks to nonlinear filtering, suppresses noise in the signal much more strongly than conventional methods. This allows the specular reflection component to be intentionally amplified, and, working in tandem with a traditional lidar, accurately calibrates phase measurements. This approach is akin to picking out a faint echo in a noisy room: extraneous sounds are cut off, making the soft reply discernible.

title: Почему квантовые системы помнят своё прошлое?
oneliner: Учёные нашли квантовые состояния, которые не забывают свою начальную конфигурацию.
description: В квантовом мире обычно всё перемешивается и забывает прошлое. Но в некоторых системах, где соседние частицы не могут одновременно возбуждаться, возникают редкие состояния — квантовые шрамы. Они хранят память о начальном порядке даже в хаосе глобальной динамики. Это открытие помогает понять, как лок
abstract: The PXP model describes atom chains where a ban on neighbor excitation drives most states to thermal equilibrium, yet quirky 'quantum scars' sidestep it. We explored how local memory persists using fidelity and local observables. Turns out, θ-symmetric and blockaded states clutch onto local memory tightly: fidelity hugs 100%, and fluctuations dwindle as the system grows. This unveils non-ergodic regimes holding local memory even amid global complexity—like islands of stability in a chaotic ocean.

title: Секрет бабочки: как физики раскрасили сложнейший узор
oneliner: Загадочный фрактальный узор «бабочка Коҳмото» наконец-то удалось разбить на пронумерованные зоны — как в детской раскраске.
description: Бабочка Коҳмото — самоподобный узор, который проявляется в поведении электронов в особых условиях. Долгое время его не удавалось разделить на независимые части: традиционные методы давали сбой. Новый подход превращает бабочку в спектральную раскраску: типы колебаний сортируются, словно краски в пали
abstract: Spectra of the Kohomoto model form a fractal phase diagram — the so-called 'Kohomoto butterfly'. Topological invariants (characteristics that don't change under continuous deformations) like Chern numbers didn't work here due to the discontinuous potential. The authors solved this problem by representing the butterfly as a spectral tree, reflecting the quasiperiodic nature through periodic spectra. This allowed them to fully classify the invariants, color the phase diagram, and open a new perspective on similar 'spectral butterflies'.

title: Может ли гравитация запутывать частицы?
oneliner: Новый анализ показывает: классическая гравитация не способна создать квантовую запутанность — и это меняет стратегию поисков квантовой природы пространства-времени.
description: Физики выяснили: если гравитация подчиняется только классическим законам, она не сможет запутать две частицы. Если же в эксперименте запутанность произойдёт, значит, действует что-то иное — тёмная материя, новые поля или неизвестные частицы. Это превращает тесты квантовой гравитации в поиск неожидан
abstract: In a recent debate over gravitationally induced entanglement (GIE), a Newton-Cartan analysis showed: classical gravity, like a stage, cannot be the source of nonlocal quantum correlations. If GIE is detected, it means something beyond ordinary gravity was involved in the experiment. Such a result would not only confirm the quantum nature of gravity, but would also rule out its purely classical description.
